AddEntityTypeOp.description:
Declare a new entity type. Idempotent: a no-op if the name already exists.
Pair with `widen_relation` (in the same request, ordered before it) to add
a brand-new type to an existing relation's domain or range.

name.description: Display name of the new entity type.

AddPropertyOp.description:
Declare a new typed scalar property field (e.g. `status`, `dueDate`,
`budget`) so a commit's `entity_properties` can write it on a live graph.
Additive: every existing record stays valid and no migration is needed.
Unlike `define_ontology` (which recreates the graph ontology), this bumps
the ontology version and registers just the field. Idempotent — a no-op
if a field with this name already exists (the existing type is kept).

allowed_values.description:
Optional **enforced** allowed-value set (a SHACL `sh:in`): a commit
whose value for this field is outside the set is rejected at write
time, so a controlled vocabulary is a one-line declaration instead of
a separate shapes graph. Only for enumerable types
(`keyword`/`text`/`i64`/`bool`); rejected on `f64`/`date_time`/`bytes`.
Use `set_property_constraint` to tighten an existing field.

name.description:
Display name of the new property field (case-insensitive; a commit's
`entity_properties[].field` resolves to it).

required.description: Advisory required flag recorded on the field (not enforced on commit).

value_type.description:
`bool` | `i64` | `f64` | `date_time` | `keyword` | `text` (default) |
`bytes`. The commit path rejects values whose type doesn't match.

AddRelationOp.description:
Declare a new relation type (e.g. `HAS_PHASE`, `ASSIGNED_TO`) with its own
domain and range, by name. The named domain/range entity types must
already exist (add them earlier in the same request with `add_entity_type`
if new). Idempotent: a no-op if a relation with this name already exists —
use `widen_relation` to grow an existing relation's domain/range. Optional
fields default exactly as the ontology spec importer does.

domain.description: Entity-type names allowed as the source (domain).

name.description: Display name of the new relation.

range.description: Entity-type names allowed as the target (range).

symmetric.description: Whether the relation is symmetric (default false).

transitive.description: Whether the relation is transitive (default false).

AdditiveOntologyEvolveOp.description: Governed proposal operation restricted to additive ontology changes.

AdditiveOntologyEvolveRequest.description: Additive-only ontology proposal helper for structured-output systems.

AnalyticBinding.description: One solution: each query variable bound to a resolved entity.

AnalyticCombinator.description:
A group-graph-pattern combinator folded over an analytic query's base BGP —
the SPARQL operators beyond a plain conjunctive join. Each carries its own
basic graph pattern (the right side); combinators apply left-to-right in the
order given. Externally tagged (`{"union": [ … ]}`, `{"optional": [ … ]}`, …).

oneOf.description: `{ base } UNION { patterns }` — multiset union of the two solution sets.

union.description: `{ base } UNION { patterns }` — multiset union of the two solution sets.

oneOf.description:
`base OPTIONAL { patterns }` — left join; unmatched base rows are kept with
the optional variables unbound.

optional.description:
`base OPTIONAL { patterns }` — left join; unmatched base rows are kept with
the optional variables unbound.

oneOf.description:
`base MINUS { patterns }` — drop base rows compatible with a right solution
they share a variable with.

minus.description:
`base MINUS { patterns }` — drop base rows compatible with a right solution
they share a variable with.

oneOf.description:
`base FILTER EXISTS { patterns }` — keep base rows with a compatible right
solution (a semijoin).

exists.description:
`base FILTER EXISTS { patterns }` — keep base rows with a compatible right
solution (a semijoin).

oneOf.description:
`base FILTER NOT EXISTS { patterns }` — keep base rows with no compatible
right solution.

not_exists.description:
`base FILTER NOT EXISTS { patterns }` — keep base rows with no compatible
right solution.

AnalyticQueryRequest.description:
A basic-graph-pattern query: a conjunction of triple patterns evaluated over
the graph's current edges (joined on shared variables), served from the
object-storage permutation view.

as_of_commit_seq.description:
Snapshot pin: evaluate the query as of this `commit_seq`, hiding any event
committed later. Errors if it exceeds the head. Forces the snapshot reduce
path — the head-fresh permutation view and the count fast paths are
declined, since they cannot reconstruct an earlier snapshot.

combinators.description:
Group-graph-pattern combinators (UNION / OPTIONAL / MINUS / EXISTS /
NOT EXISTS) folded over the base `patterns`, left-to-right. Empty (the
default) leaves the query a plain conjunctive BGP.

oneOf.description:
Read consistency (A3 serving; A5 default). `eventual` (the **default**)
serves ENTIRELY from the last published segment RDF family (the
maintenance-owned, asserted-only default) at its watermark — no in-memory
gap fold, so the query stays available while background indexing lags — and
surfaces the served watermark on `snapshot.served_at_seq`. Eventual serving
requires a published dataset; a graph with none yet returns a retryable 429.
`strong` (explicit opt-in) evaluates against the head snapshot on the exact
conformant engine, folding any un-indexed WAL tail in memory — the path that
carries `rdfs:subClassOf` closure over the aggregation.

limit.description: Cap the number of returned bindings.

max_fetched_bytes.description:
Companion byte ceiling on block bytes fetched by the query. Clamped to the
server's `MAX_ANALYTIC_FETCHED_BYTES`; also trips `truncated_by_read_budget`.

max_object_reads.description:
Fail-closed ceiling on object reads (GETs) the query's scans may issue —
the per-request object-storage cost cap. Clamped to the server's
`MAX_ANALYTIC_OBJECT_READS`; when hit, the scan stops and the response's
`truncated_by_read_budget` flag is set.

max_solutions.description:
Fail-closed ceiling on the intermediate join solution set. Clamped to the
server's `MAX_ANALYTIC_SOLUTIONS`; when the join hits it the result is
partial and the response's `truncated` flag is set. Distinct from `limit`,
which is a caller-requested cap on the final, complete result.

oneOf.description:
A5 read-your-writes floor: require the read to be served from state whose
watermark ≥ this `commit_seq` (the `new_commit_seq` a commit/import
returned). Under the default `eventual`, a floor not yet covered by the
published dataset returns a retryable `read_your_writes_pending` 429 (poll
until it lands); under `strong` the floor is satisfied by construction and a
floor above head is a client error. `None` == no floor.

truncated.description:
True when a safety cap (the intermediate-solution ceiling) cut the join —
the solution set is partial. Not set by `limit`, which is caller-requested.

truncated_by_read_budget.description:
True when the per-request object-read budget (`max_object_reads` /
`max_fetched_bytes`) was hit, so scans stopped early — the result may be
incomplete.

AnalyticTerm.description:
A triple-pattern term: a named query variable (`{"var": "x"}`) or a fixed
entity (`{"entity": {...}}`).

AnalyticTriplePattern.description:
One triple pattern of an analytic (BGP) query. The predicate is a relation
name, resolved server-side. In an inference rule body the predicate may also
be the reserved [`RDF_TYPE_PREDICATE`] for a type-membership constraint.

id.description: Entity id or edge-event id, as a string.

AskExplain.description:
Per-request instrumentation of the `/v1/ask` pipeline: what each stage cost,
how much the DB narrowed the world before the model saw it, and the embedding
model that did the grounding.

embedding.description:
The embedding model that grounded + retrieved — its id, or `lexical` for
the hash/lexical fallback when the graph has no managed embedding model.

narrowing.description:
How much the DB narrowed: the vocabulary the question snapped to and the
size of the retrieved evidence set the answer rests on.

timings.description: Wall-clock latency of each stage of the one call, in milliseconds.

oneOf.description: With `corrected`: the plan that should have been decoded.

accepted.description:
True when the verdict was captured. False when signal capture is off
on this deployment — the request contract is identical either way, so
clients never break when the flag flips.

AskFeedbackVerdict.description:
A user verdict on one ask (`POST /v1/ask/feedback`) — the planner's
explicit feedback capture. Joined to the ask's `ask_trace` signal by
`ask_id`.

candidates.description:
Real vocabulary the question snapped to (classes/relations/properties/
terms) — a subset of the graph's vocabulary by construction.

constrained.description:
True when identifier slots were hard-constrained during decode. Always
false in `grounding_only` (no decode); true under the resident planner.

signature_forced.description:
True when the grounding forced a single relation via a type signature
(WS10). Always false in `grounding_only` v1; set by the resident planner.

AskNarrowing.description:
How much the database narrowed the world before the model — the core
context-substrate quantity, exposed per request.

vocab_candidates.description:
Vocabulary terms the question snapped to (classes/relations/properties) —
the narrowed candidate set, a subset of real vocabulary by construction.

retryable.description: True when retrying after the planner recovers can produce a plan.

oneOf.description:
Typed explanation when execution fell back without a validated V2
plan. For every executed ask exactly one of `structured_v2` and
`plan_failure` is present.

query.description: The retrieval query text that was executed.

oneOf.description:
The structured plan the resident model decoded under the shortlist
trie, when the constrained leg ran. Identifiers are real vocabulary by
construction (server-verified ⊆ the grounded shortlist).

Complete versioned execution contract. Present for every constrained
planner result; V1 remains as a compatibility projection.

as_of_commit_seq.description:
Snapshot pin (transaction-time ceiling): retrieval and citations
reproduce the graph as of this commit sequence; the response
`snapshot.as_of_commit_seq` echoes the pin. Omitted means head.

as_of_valid_time.description:
Valid-time cursor (RFC 3339): retrieval reflects facts true at this
instant. Omitted means the latest valid time.

execute.description:
Run retrieval and return citations (default true). When false, only the
grounding is computed — the caller drives its own retrieval/model.

fields.description: Entity attributes retained as evidence on the internal execution trace.

oneOf.description:
Authorization/attribute filter applied to grounding prepasses and final
execution at the same snapshot.

snapshot_token.description:
Opaque alternative to `as_of_commit_seq`. Exactly one pin spelling may
be supplied; grounding, validation, execution, trace, and citations all
use the resolved commit.

top_k.description: Max citations to return (default 8, clamped to 25).

answer.description:
A synthesized answer when a model produced one; in `grounding_only` this
is a templated evidence summary (the retrieved matches), not model prose.

ask_id.description:
Server-stamped id for this ask. Reference it in
`POST /v1/ask/feedback` to attach a verdict; when signal capture is on
it is also the `request_id` joining the emitted `ask_trace` signal to
its `ask_feedback` — the planner fine-tune's training join key.

citations.description: Evidence: the graph items the answer rests on.

confidence.description: Confidence in [0, 1], from the top retrieved score.

explain.description:
Instrumentation for the one call: per-stage latency, how much the DB
narrowed, and which embedding model grounded the question. Makes the
"DB narrows, cheap model decodes" pipeline observable per request.

grounding.description: How the question was mapped onto real vocabulary.

oneOf.description: The retrieval that was executed (absent when `execute` is false).

AskStructuredQuery.description:
The constrained structured plan: the entity type the answer set is
restricted to (the QALD-class-task shape), plus — when the question admits
them — a relation and an anchor entity. Every slot is real vocabulary by
construction (decoded under the shortlist trie or forced by a type
signature, and server-verified ⊆ the shortlist either way).

target_type.description:
Decoded target entity type (a declared class, subtype-inclusive at
execution).

AskTimings.description: Per-stage wall-clock latency for one `/v1/ask` call, in milliseconds.

decode_ms.description:
Shortlist-constrained structured-query decode on the resident model;
absent when the constrained leg did not run.

ground_ms.description: Grounding the question to real vocabulary (WS11 resolve).

retrieve_ms.description: Hybrid retrieval against the pinned snapshot. Zero when `execute` is false.

synth_ms.description: Resident-model synthesis; absent when no planner ran (`grounding_only`).

total_ms.description: End-to-end latency of the whole call.

CalibrationServingDefaults.description:
Calibration serving default derived from a promoted `calibration` run:
the Platt scaler the retrieval-premise producer applies when a request
supplies no inline `calibration`. The scaler is fit on the BM25 entity
channel (the same channel query-driven premise requests use) from
execution-verified outcomes.

model_id.description: The retrieval channel the scaler was fitted on.

run.description: The promoted registry run this scaler came from.

snapshot_commit_seq.description:
The snapshot the scaler was fitted at (its split pin — provenance;
the producer re-pins the manifest to the served snapshot).

CapturedSignal.description:
One captured signal as the range reader returns it: the stored envelope
plus the commit seq of the object it was flushed at (the temporal-split
coordinate trainers filter on).

last_commit_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the head commit.

source_id.description:
Stable entity id of the source — names are neither unique nor stable,
so consumers mirroring the graph join on the ids.

target_id.description: Stable entity id of the target (see `source_id`).

oneOf.description:
Write-time validation outcome (roadmap item 9 M0). Present only when the
commit raised at least one warning; absent on a fully-conformant commit.
M0 reports structural domain/range mismatches in `warn` mode (never
blocks); `reject` mode and SHACL shapes arrive with the constraints work.

drain_pressure.description:
R27d(1) advisory pacing telemetry: the WAL drain pressure at ack time as a
`0.0`–`1.0` tail-fill ratio. Present only once the tail is over half full.
Clients SHOULD reduce write concurrency as this rises; advisory only.

oneOf.description:
Active graph-schema SHACL validation outcome. Present only when the graph
has an activated schema shape set and the commit violated or warned on it.

throttle_ms.description:
R27d(1) advisory pacing telemetry: the pacing latency this write ack
absorbed — head-lane serialization plus admission wait — in whole
milliseconds. Present only past a noise floor (a fast commit omits it) and
mirrored to the `X-LBB-Throttle-Ms` response header. Clients SHOULD reduce
write concurrency as this rises; it is never load-bearing for correctness.

written_properties.description:
Property fields actually written by this commit, per entity (canonical
field names, resolved against the ontology). Lets a writer confirm a
property landed without a follow-up read. Present only on a fresh commit
that carried `entity_properties`; empty on idempotent replays and no-ops.

ConformanceReport.description:
Closed-world validation report for a commit. Mirrors the shape of a SHACL
`sh:ValidationReport`: `conforms` plus one `results` entry per violation.

focus_node.description: The focus node the constraint was evaluated on (the entity name/id).

value.description: The offending value (the endpoint's entity-type name).

as_of_commit_seq.description:
Snapshot pin: reproduce the state as of this `commit_seq`, hiding any
event committed later. Errors if it exceeds the current head. Omit for
the latest snapshot.

DateBucketGranularity.description:
The calendar unit a [`SparqlGroupKey::DateBucket`] truncates a datetime to.
Truncation is in UTC; the label is the bucket's start as a sortable string.

type.description:
Entity type — the type signature narrows the relation on this. Optional:
omit it to have the database recover the type by resolving `name` to a
real entity (exact match, most-connected on a cross-type name collision),
so a caller that knows a name but not its type can still decode. The
entity it resolved to is echoed in the response's `resolved_source` /
`resolved_target`.

use_model_when_forced.description:
Call the model even when the type signature forces exactly one relation
(default false — a forced pair is answered by the DB alone, no model).

DecodeResolved.description:
The entity a type-less [`DecodeEndpoint`] resolved to — the type the database
recovered from the bare name, and how many other entities shared that name.

other_candidates.description:
Count of other entities that carry this exact name under a different type
(0 when the name was unambiguous). The highest-degree match was chosen.

candidates.description:
The relations the DB admits for this type pair — the narrowed vocabulary
the decode is constrained to.

gen_ms.description: Model generation latency in ms, present only when the model was called.

relation.description:
The decoded relation. `None` only in `grounding_only` (no decoder
configured and the pair does not force a single relation).

oneOf.description:
The entity the source resolved to, present only when the source endpoint
was given without a `type` (the DB recovered it from the name).

The entity the target resolved to, present only when the target endpoint
was given without a `type`.

signature_forced.description: True when the type pair admits exactly one relation (the DB forces it).

DecorationStatus.description:
One row of the engine's decoration-status catalog: whether a declarable
ontology decoration is actually acted on by the engine (`enforced`), recorded
and meaningful but not the mechanism (`advisory`), or present-but-unwired
(`reserved`). The catalog is engine-global — identical for every graph — so
the API, MCP, and console agree on what the engine *acts on* versus what it
merely *remembers*. See `decoration_catalog`.

effect.description: One line: what the engine does with it (or that it does nothing yet).

DerivedEdgeView.description: One derived edge (a fact that follows from the rules but is not asserted).

confidence.description:
Confidence inherited from the supporting facts: the **max** over
derivations of the **min** over each derivation's premises (so a fact is
as strong as its weakest premise, and the best derivation wins).

depends_on.description:
The provenance trail this fact transitively rests on — typed leaves so an
asserted edge (`Asserted`) and a calibrated retrieval match (`Retrieval`)
stay distinguishable and a retrieval match can never masquerade as an
asserted fact (FAL-PROV-NOT-LAUNDERED). Pure rule derivation resolves to
`Asserted` leaves only.

rule.description: The rule that first derived this edge.

include_raw_rerank.description:
Append a raw-f32 region per cluster so persisted vector search can opt
into exact rerank (`rerank` on the search request). Larger objects at
rest; default off keeps runs compact and 4-bit-only.

quantizer.description:
Quantizer codec for the persisted cluster blocks. Absent / `None` uses
the default `tq-hadamard` (4-bit). Set `exrabitq` (with `quantizer_bits`,
2..=8) for the higher-bit Extended RaBitQ codec that can reach
rerank-grade recall without the raw-f32 region (P30/R9). The scheme is
self-describing in the persisted index meta, so reads dispatch
automatically; this only changes how new runs are built.

quantizer_bits.description:
Bits per dimension for the `exrabitq` scheme (2..=8). Ignored by
`tq-hadamard` (fixed 4-bit); defaults to 4 when `quantizer` is set
without bits.

reranked_candidates.description:
Number of candidates re-scored with exact raw-f32 distances. Zero when
`rerank` was not requested or the run has no raw region.

oneOf.description:
Which persisted ANN run served this query relative to the resolved
`as_of` ceiling. Present only when `explain` is set. See
[`SearchTemporalCoverage`].

as_of_commit_seq.description:
Snapshot pin (transaction-time ceiling): results hide every event
committed after this sequence, reproducing that past snapshot. The ANN
run is selected as the newest run at or below the pin and the pin bounds
the forward overlay; candidate generation is never the correctness layer.
Omitted means the current head.

as_of_valid_time.description:
Valid-time cursor (RFC 3339): results reflect facts true at this
instant. Omitted means the latest valid time.

oneOf.description:
A5 read-your-writes floor: require the read to be served from state whose
watermark ≥ this `commit_seq` (the `new_commit_seq` a commit/import
returned). Under the default `Eventual`, a floor not yet covered returns a
retryable `read_your_writes_pending` 429. `None` == no floor. See
`SearchEngineOptions::min_indexed_seq`.

query_vector.description:
Client-computed query vector. When present it is used directly and the
provider only identifies the embedding space; `query` remains for
display/explain purposes.

rerank.description:
Opt into exact raw-f32 reranking of the persisted vector candidates.
Only effective when the run carries a raw-f32 region (built with
`include_raw`); otherwise it is a no-op. Default off keeps the compact
4-bit-approximate path.

rerank_top_k.description:
How many top approximate candidates to re-score exactly before
truncating to `top_k`. Defaults to a small multiple of `top_k` when
unset; ignored unless `rerank` is set.

attributes.description:
The entity's typed scalar properties as native JSON — the flat read-back
of what a commit's `entity_properties` wrote, so round-trip verification
is a one-hop lookup. A numeric property reads back as a JSON number, a
bool as a bool, a datetime as an RFC3339 string; empty when the entity
carries no scalar attributes. The `/entities` list returns the same
`attributes` shape under `?fields=`.

entity_id.description:
Optional resolved entity id (32-char hex). When set, the embedding attaches
to exactly this entity, bypassing `(type, key, name)` resolution. This is
the identity a managed backfill has for each entity it enumerates from a
snapshot — it holds the id but not the original external `key`, which the
id is a one-way hash of, so name-based resolution would create a phantom
name-keyed twin instead of attaching to the keyed entity. `entity_id`
takes precedence over `key`/`name`; `type` is still resolved for the
entity record.

key.description:
Optional stable external key, matching [`NamedEntityInput::key`]. Set it to
attach this embedding to a keyed entity; omit it for `(type, name)`
identity.

oneOf.description: Defaults to Cosine.

attributes.description:
Typed scalar attributes (native JSON), present only when the request asks
for a projection via `?fields=a,b` (or `?fields=*` for all). Lets "list
entities and their titles" be one call instead of a list + N point
lookups. Same `attributes` shape the single-entity read returns.

EntityFieldsWritten.description: The property fields written to one entity by a commit (canonical names).

removed_fields.description:
Previously-stored fields this write **dropped** — present (and non-empty)
only under `property_merge: "replace"`, where the commit's field list
becomes the entity's whole attribute set. Under the default `merge` it is
always empty (a merge never removes a field), so a populated
`removed_fields` is the explicit signal that a destructive replace pruned
attributes the commit did not re-send.

fields.description:
Typed entity properties to return on each matched row. Empty keeps the
lean row; `"*"` or `"all"` projects every property.

filter.description: Property/metadata predicate evaluated against one loaded graph snapshot.

limit.description:
Maximum rows returned. The response's `matched_count` still reports the
full number of matches before this limit is applied.

matched_count.description: Total entities satisfying the predicate before `limit` is applied.

oneOf.description: Highest commit covered by the ranged adjacency family.

Highest commit covered by the **vector/ANN index**, or `None` when no ANN
index has been built. See [`Self::bm25_indexed_commit_seq`].

Highest commit covered by the **BM25 full-text index**, or `None` when no
full-text index has been built. Together with `ann_indexed_commit_seq`
this answers "is this graph indexed for search?" on the dedicated entity
metadata diagnostics read — unlike `snapshot.compacted_seq`, which is the
graph snapshot base. Ordinary entity detail leaves derived-index lineage
absent so a point read never blocks on index discovery. Lag behind
`snapshot.commit_seq` is the unindexed tail.

as_of_commit_seq.description:
Snapshot pin: reproduce the neighborhood as of this `commit_seq`, hiding
any event committed later. Errors if it exceeds the current head, and
forces the snapshot scan (the ranged adjacency fast path declines a
pinned read). Omit for the latest snapshot.

EntityNeighborhoodResponse.description:
Result of an entity point lookup: the entity plus its current out/in
neighborhood. Served from the ranged adjacency run's per-entity slices when
one exists (cost is the entity's degree, not the whole graph), otherwise
from the snapshot overlay.

served_from_ranged.description:
True when served from the persisted ranged adjacency run (the
point-lookup fast path); false when computed from the snapshot overlay.

EntityPropertiesInput.description:
Literal property values to attach to one entity (matched by type + name).

`properties` accepts **two interchangeable shapes**:
- verbose — a list of `{ "field": "...", "value": { "<type>": v } }` objects,
  where `<type>` is one of `bool|i64|f64|date_time|keyword|text`; and
- flat — a `{ "field": value }` object, e.g. `{ "h_index": 52, "title": "VP" }`,
  where the value type is inferred from JSON (bool/integer/float/string) and
  then coerced to the field's declared type at commit (so a flat string lands
  in a `keyword` field, a flat `"2026-06-26"` in a `date_time` field, and a
  flat integer in an `f64` field).

The flat shape removes the nested-`value` ceremony that made `entity_properties`
hard to get right; reach for the verbose shape only when you need to force a
specific wire type.

entity_id.description:
Optional resolved entity id (32-char hex). When set, these properties
attach to exactly this entity, bypassing `(type, key, name)` resolution —
the same escape hatch [`EntityEmbeddingInput::entity_id`] provides. A
managed backfill enumerating a snapshot holds each entity's id but not the
original external `key` (the id is a one-way hash of it), so resolving by
name would attach to — or mint — a phantom name-keyed twin instead of the
real keyed entity. `entity_id` takes precedence over `key`/`name`; `type`
is still resolved for the entity record.

key.description:
Optional stable external key, matching [`NamedEntityInput::key`]. Set it to
attach these properties to a keyed entity; omit it for `(type, name)`
identity.

remove_fields.description:
Field names to **drop** from this entity, applied after `properties` in the
same commit — a surgical per-field delete that avoids a replace-mode
re-send of the whole survivor set (the silent-wipe footgun). Works in
either `property_merge` mode: the named fields are removed from the
entity's stored set even when the rest of the commit merges. A field
listed in both `properties` and `remove_fields` is rejected (you cannot
both set and drop it); an unknown field name is rejected. Dropped fields
are echoed back under `written_properties[].removed_fields`.

as_of_commit_seq.description:
Snapshot pin: trace the transitions as they had been recorded by this
`commit_seq`. Errors if it exceeds the current head.

relation.description: The state/status relation to trace, e.g. `IN_STAGE` or `HAS_STATUS`.

is_state_relation.description:
True when the relation keeps a single active value per source, so this
sequence is a genuine state machine (each entry supersedes the prior).
False (append-only/multi-active) means the entries co-exist and the dwell
figures are between recordings rather than between exclusive states.

EntityTypeSampleResponse.description:
Exact class cardinality plus a bounded deterministic sample served from the
ranged adjacency meta. `indexed_commit_seq` makes index lag explicit while
`snapshot.commit_seq` reports the current graph head.

EntityTypeSampleRow.description:
One bounded, index-backed class member for interactive graph exploration.
Unlike [`EntityExplorerRow`], this deliberately carries only fields the
adjacency build already knows; exhaustive attributes and observation counts
remain on the full entity/browse reads.

observation_id.description:
Stable observation id used by full-fidelity export/import. Ordinary
callers omit it and receive the existing deterministic request id.

oneOf.description:
Optional page-region anchor for this evidence. Requires `text`
(the region anchors to the evidence observation the text creates).

ExtractorDatasetResponse.description:
`GET /v1/models/extractor-dataset` — the extractor fine-tune's training
feed: stored `EPISODE` transcripts joined (via evidence observations) to
the facts the observe pipeline committed from them, plus the ontology
vocabulary the serving prompt carries (so the trainer rebuilds the exact
extraction prompt).

relations.description: The extraction prompt's relation vocabulary at this snapshot.

split_seq.description: Facts with commit seq ≤ this were consumed.

types.description: The extraction prompt's entity-type vocabulary at this snapshot.

ExtractorExample.description:
One extractor training example: an episode transcript and the facts that
were actually committed from it.

ExtractorFact.description:
One extractor training fact, mirroring the resident extractor's strict
output JSON (`{statement, source_type, source, relation, target_type,
target, confidence}`) — every committed fact was vocabulary-snapped and
branch-gated at observe time, so labels are execution-verified.

oneOf.description:
Which persisted run served this query relative to the resolved `as_of`
ceiling, and how far the forward overlay had to reach. Present only when
`explain` is set. See [`SearchTemporalCoverage`].

as_of_commit_seq.description:
Snapshot pin (transaction-time ceiling): results hide every event
committed after this sequence, reproducing that past snapshot. The BM25
run is selected as the newest run at or below the pin and the pin bounds
the forward overlay; candidate generation is never the correctness layer.
Omitted means the current head.

as_of_valid_time.description:
Valid-time cursor (RFC 3339): results reflect facts true at this
instant. Omitted means the latest valid time.

oneOf.description:
A5 read-your-writes floor: require the read to be served from state whose
watermark ≥ this `commit_seq` (the `new_commit_seq` a commit/import
returned). Under the default `Eventual`, a floor not yet covered returns a
retryable `read_your_writes_pending` 429. `None` == no floor. See
`SearchEngineOptions::min_indexed_seq`.

offset.description:
Number of leading results to skip after ordering, for stateless paging:
`offset = page * top_k` walks pages deterministically. Defaults to 0.
Paging operates over a bounded candidate pool (~16 × `top_k`), not the
full match set, so paging is lossy past that pool: an `offset` beyond the
pool returns an empty page and documents outside the pool are omitted.
Keep `top_k` large enough that the pages you need fall inside the pool.

order.description:
Sort direction for `order_by`. Defaults to descending (highest score /
metadata value first).

order_by.description:
Ordering key for the result page. Defaults to relevance score. Ties
always break on the stable document id so paging is deterministic.
Ordering is applied over the bounded candidate pool (~16 × `top_k`), so
`order_by: metadata` reorders the top-scoring slice rather than the full
match set — matches outside the pool are not considered.

FusionServingDefaults.description:
Fusion serving default derived from a promoted `fusion` run: the weights
the search path applies when a request neither overrides the field nor
pins an explicit retrieval profile.

run.description: The promoted registry run these weights came from.

GovernedConflictAggregationRequest.description:
Snapshot-pinned governed fact-conflict aggregation. Authorization is a
required filter and is evaluated before any grouping or value collection.

truncated.description: Scan or output-group ceiling made the result incomplete.

GraphAnchor.description:
A structural co-bind constraint: restrict (or boost) the text/vector
candidates to entities within an anchor entity's relation-neighborhood. This
is the engine-native form of the query-conditional channel routing validated
in the Stage-G ablation (quality-ledger R17) — "documents about X AND in the
same group as Y" becomes one snapshot-consistent search rather than a blind
list fusion. The neighborhood is computed by a bounded, snapshot-reduced
traversal; final candidates are still re-verified against snapshot visibility.

anchor.description: The anchor entity to expand from.

hops.description: Neighborhood radius in hops (clamped to a small bound; default 2).

oneOf.description: `filter` (hard JOIN) or `boost` (soft). Defaults to `filter`.

relation.description:
Restrict the neighborhood walk to this relation type (by name). `None`
follows every relation.

GraphBindMode.description: How a graph anchor co-binds with the text/vector channels.

GraphBranchMergeRequest.description:
`POST /v1/graph/branch/merge` — replay a child branch's commits onto its
fork parent (the branch named by the request URL) as one new commit batch.
Append-only: provenance and event ids are preserved, commit seqs are
re-stamped onto the target's next seq.

delete_source.description:
Delete every object under the source branch's prefix after a successful
merge (the branch is consumed).

from_branch.description:
The child branch whose post-fork commits are replayed. It must have been
created from the branch this request is addressed to (its fork parent).

validate.description:
Run the active SHACL schema on the would-be merged state BEFORE the head
write and refuse (merged=false + report) on violations — even when the
graph's enforce mode is `report`. With no active shapes (or mode `off`)
there is nothing to validate against and this is a no-op.

commits_applied.description:
Branch-local commits replayed (0 for a no-op merge of an unchanged
branch).

conflicts.description:
Branch facts dropped by the conflict rule: a fact superseded on the
target after the fork point wins over the branch's version.

idempotent_replay.description:
True when this request id was already applied; `commits_applied` and
`conflicts` are not re-derived on a replay.

merged.description:
False when `validate` refused the merge; the target is untouched and
`validation` carries the report.

refusal.description: Why `merged` is false, when it is.

snapshot.description:
The target branch snapshot after the merge (or its untouched head on
refusal / no-op).

source_deleted.description: True when `delete_source` ran (the source branch's objects are gone).

oneOf.description:
The SHACL audit of the would-be merged state (present when shapes are
active and either `validate` was requested or violations were found).

GraphCard.description:
WS3 — a per-graph, per-snapshot self-description an agent can inject as prompt
context: the types, relations, properties, and top attribute values that
actually exist, with counts and a freshness watermark. The database writes
its own guaranteed-true few-shot examples. Computed from the ontology,
resident overlay, and BM25 meta — no doc/postings reads — and bounded by
`limits` so it stays small enough to prompt with.

GraphChangesResponse.description:
WS4 — changes since a commit sequence: an agent refreshes its context by
reading the delta, not re-querying. Records are returned in commit order and
include edge retractions/tombstones. Paging is by whole commits (`next_since`
walks the window). Segment folds repackage history rather than reduce it, so
any `since` up to the head is served; a `since` BEYOND the head (a cursor
from a wiped/re-created branch) returns `reset` and is served as HTTP 409
with body `{"error":"delta_unavailable","reset":true,"snapshot_token":…}`.

next_since.description: Pass back as `since` for the next page.

reset.description:
The cursor cannot be answered from this branch's history (`since` beyond
the head — the branch was wiped or re-created); the client must
full-read. Served as HTTP 409 with an `error: delta_unavailable` body.

to_commit_seq.description: Inclusive upper bound of this page (head unless paged/truncated).

truncated.description: More commits remained beyond this page.

GraphCommitDryRunResponse.description:
The result of a validate-only commit (`POST /v1/graph/commit?dry_run=true`):
the request is fully validated against the ontology and the active schema and
the would-be effect is reported, but nothing is persisted. A 4xx here is the
same validation error a real commit would raise (unknown type/relation,
unregistered property field, type mismatch), so it is a safe ETL preflight.

dry_run.description: Always true — marks this as a non-persisting preflight.

op_count.description: Number of WAL ops the commit would produce.

oneOf.description:
Active-schema SHACL audit of the prospective commit, when a shape bundle
is active. `conforms=false` means a `reject`-mode commit would be blocked.

skipped_edges.description: Edges that would be skipped as unchanged (idempotent no-ops).

triplets.description: Triplets that would be written, after idempotent skip-unchanged filtering.

written_properties.description:
Property fields that would be written, per entity (canonical names),
resolved against the ontology — the same echo a real commit returns.

drain_pressure.description:
R27d(1) advisory WAL drain pressure (0.0–1.0), mirrored from
`commit.drain_pressure`. Present only once the tail is over half full.

replayed.description:
Top-level replay signal for idempotency-key collisions/retries. Mirrors
`commit.idempotent_replay` so callers do not have to unwrap the commit
envelope before deciding whether anything new was written.

throttle_ms.description:
R27d(1) advisory pacing telemetry, mirrored from `commit.throttle_ms` to
the envelope top level (like `replayed`) and to the `X-LBB-Throttle-Ms`
response header. Present only past a noise floor; clients SHOULD reduce
write concurrency as it rises.

written_properties.description:
Top-level echo of property fields actually written by this request.
Empty on idempotent replays and no-ops.

as_of_commit_seq.description:
Pin the export to a past snapshot: only entities, assertions, and
observations committed at or before this `commit_seq` are emitted. A pin
past the current head is rejected loudly (never clamped), matching the
graph read paths. `None` exports the latest head. This is what makes a
long-running training run's export reproducible — resolve the head once,
then pin every subsequent read to the same `commit_seq` so an N+1 commit
mid-run can never change the training corpus.

snapshot_token.description:
Opaque, self-describing token for the exact snapshot this export reflects
(`tenant:graph:branch:epoch:commit_seq`). Records the exported snapshot's
provenance so a caller can pin subsequent reads to — and verify
reproducibility against — the same point in history.

GraphForkResponse.description:
Response to `POST /v1/graph/fork`: the fork runs as a durable background job
(a create-only copy of the source graph into a new graph id in the same
tenant), so this acknowledges the enqueue and points at the poll surface.

poll.description:
Poll the destination graph's metadata to observe the fork completing
(the destination becomes readable once its head is published).

GraphImportIndexOutcome.description:
Outcome of the optional one-shot index build a bulk import runs at the end
when `?index=true` is passed — the "bulk load, queryable on return"
primitive. `built` is whether the full build completed, so the imported data
is served from the persisted runs (not just the ephemeral snapshot fallback)
by the time the request returns. `skipped_reason` is present only when
`built` is false: `"index_busy"` (the node's build-admission gate was busy —
the data is committed and queryable, retry the build later) or
`"index_error"` (the build failed; detail is logged server-side, not
surfaced, to avoid leaking internal paths).

GraphImportLine.description:
One line of an NDJSON bulk-import stream (`POST /v1/graph/import`). Each line
is a single JSON object that is either a triplet (carries `relation`) or an
entity-properties record (carries `properties`); the two shapes are disjoint,
so the importer routes each line by shape. This is the streamable counterpart
of `TripletCommitFile`: a client streams a large dataset line-by-line instead
of buffering one giant commit, and the server batches lines into bounded
internal commits.

GraphImportLineError.description:
A per-line parse/commit failure surfaced by the bulk importer. `line` is the
1-based NDJSON line number. The importer records these and continues (skip the
bad line), unless `strict` is requested.

GraphImportResponse.description:
Aggregate outcome of a bulk NDJSON import. `committed_commit_seq` is the
sequence of the last internal batch commit (absent if nothing committed).
`errors` is bounded; `error_count` is the true total even when the list is
truncated. `index` is present only when the caller passed `?index=true`.

idempotency_key.description: The caller key, or the server-minted key when the caller omitted one.

idempotent_replay.description:
True when every internal mutation batch was the already-committed
receipt for this idempotency key.

mutation_receipt_id.description:
Stable identity for the whole multi-batch mutation. Replays return this
exact receipt and the original terminal commit sequence.

observations.description: Exact observation records imported from a full-fidelity export.

GraphListResponse.description: The live, head-backed graphs under a tenant (one stack in SaaS mode).

index_caught_up.description:
One-shot "has the persisted index caught up to head?" signal, so a
bulk-import caller does not hand-assemble the three-field predicate.
`Some(true)` iff both the BM25 and ANN persisted runs are current to the
head commit (no un-indexed tail); `Some(false)` when a build is still
pending or absent; `None` when indexes were not inspected
(`include_indexes=false`). Note (Strong consistency): committed data is
queryable via the per-query overlay *before* this turns true — this marks
the persisted fast path being current, not queryability.

segment_count.description: Compacted snapshot segments referenced by the graph manifest.

temporal_coverage.description:
Temporal coverage of this snapshot — what kinds of as-of query the data
can actually answer. Lets a caller tell *before* querying whether
point-in-time / daily views are possible, instead of discovering that
every as-of returns the same picture.

temporal_coverage_computed.description:
Whether `temporal_coverage` was computed from the full snapshot. The
bounded HTTP metadata path is head-only by default; callers that need
exact event-time coverage opt in explicitly.

oneOf.description:
Unified visibility-manifest summary. `None` before the first family is
published or when index inspection was disabled.

GraphRdfImportError.description:
A bounded parse error from RDF bulk import. The RDF parser reports syntax
positions in its message, so this type keeps the wire shape honest instead of
pretending there is always a recoverable line number.

GraphRdfImportPredicate.description:
A predicate discovered while importing RDF through `POST /v1/graph/import/rdf`.
`uri` is the original RDF predicate IRI. `relation` is the fixed
`RDF_TRIPLE` relation used for committed edges, while `lbb_predicate_iri`
remains the source predicate IRI that the RDF/SPARQL projection emits.
`property` and `lbb_property_iri` are retained as nullable compatibility
fields for older clients and are not populated by the fixed-schema importer.

GraphRdfImportResponse.description:
Aggregate outcome of an RDF import. Every RDF statement is committed
through a fixed `RDF_TRIPLE` edge; source predicate and literal term metadata
are stored on the edge so the RDF/SPARQL projection can emit source RDF terms
while avoiding one ontology relation/property per source predicate.

GraphReloadResponse.description:
Outcome of `POST /v1/graph/reload` — the declarative "make the graph match
this full dataset" verb. One atomic cutover replaces the whole current state:
records present in the payload are upserted, and entities present at the
pre-reload head but absent from the payload are removed from current state
(retraction semantics — history stays queryable via `?as_of_commit_seq=`).
The delta report is the "prove I don't have wrong data" aid.

The whole reconciliation lands as a single graph-head advance, so a reader
never observes a mixed old/new state: a snapshot pinned at `prior_commit_seq`
sees only the old state, and `new_commit_seq` sees only the new state, with
nothing in between.

dry_run.description:
True when this was a `?dry_run=true` preview: nothing durable changed and
the counts + `new_commit_seq`/`new_snapshot_token` are the *proposed*
effect of a real reload against the same pre-reload head.

edges_added.description: Payload edges not currently present, within the reconciled scopes.

edges_changed.description: Payload edges already present (re-asserted), within the reconciled scopes.

edges_removed.description: Current edges retracted because an endpoint left current state.

entities_added.description: Entities present in the payload but not at the pre-reload head.

entities_changed.description:
Entities present in both the payload and the pre-reload head (re-asserted;
this is a presence intersection, not a deep property diff).

entities_removed.description:
Entities present at the pre-reload head but absent from the payload — the
ones leaving current state.

error_count.description:
Parse errors for malformed payload lines (bounded; `error_count` is the
true total). Mirrors bulk import.

graph_created.description:
True when the target graph did not exist and this reload bootstrapped it
(the pre-reload baseline was empty).

idempotency_key.description:
The idempotency key that scopes this reload's single commit (the caller
key, or a server-minted one).

idempotent_replay.description:
True when this request id was already applied (idempotent replay): the
diff counts are reported as zero because nothing new was written.

new_commit_seq.description:
Commit sequence after cutover (real), or the proposed next sequence (dry
run).

new_snapshot_token.description: Snapshot token after cutover (real), or the proposed token (dry run).

no_op.description:
True when the desired state already matched the current state and no
commit was written (real mode only).

prior_commit_seq.description:
Commit sequence of the pre-reload head — the diff baseline and the
rollback anchor.

prior_snapshot_token.description:
Snapshot token of the pre-reload head. Read it back with
`?as_of_commit_seq=<prior_commit_seq>` to see the old state — the rollback
hint.

removed_sample.description:
A bounded sample of the entities leaving current state, as `type/name`
display identities (external keys are one-way-hashed and unrecoverable) —
a sanity aid for confirming the reload drops what you expect.

GraphRetractRequest.description:
Retract specific edges and/or every edge touching given entities. Graph facts
are append-only; a retraction does not erase events but appends a retracting
event that supersedes the matched current edges, so they drop out of
current-state reads (and re-appear in an `as_of` read before the retraction).
This is the surgical alternative to the whole-graph reset for fixing a
wrongly-added fact — e.g. an auto-extracted entity that turned out to be noise.

edges.description:
Specific edges to retract, matched against current edges by
(source, relation, target).

entities.description:
Entities whose every current edge (as source or target) is retracted —
effectively tombstoning the entity from current-state reads. The entity
record itself is retained (append-only) and stays visible in `as_of` reads.

purge_entity_properties.description:
Also clear every stored scalar property (`text`, `acl`, …) of each entity
in `entities`, so the entity has **no current presence** and fully leaves
search AND SPARQL in this one call — the "delete this record" primitive.
Without it, `retract` tombstones edges only; a property-carrying entity
(e.g. a content chunk with an `acl`) stays searchable and ACL-filterable
because presence is `live-edge OR non-empty properties`. Append-only: the
records survive in `as_of` reads; only the current property set is cleared
(a later `commit` can repopulate it). No-op for `edges`-only requests.

edge_event_ids.description: The retracting edge-event ids appended by this request.

idempotent_replay.description:
True when this request id was already applied (idempotent replay); counts
are reported as zero because nothing new was written.

no_op.description: True when nothing matched, so no event was written.

purged_entities.description:
Entities (from `entities`) whose stored properties were cleared by
`purge_entity_properties`. Zero when the flag is unset.

retracted_edges.description: Distinct current edges retracted by this request.

retracted_entities.description: Entities (from `entities`) that had at least one current edge retracted.

branches.description: Branches that exist under this graph (e.g. `["main"]`).

GroundabilityReport.description:
WS13 — a graph's self-reported groundability: per graph, which completion
mechanisms will carry. Turns the campaign's hand measurements into a
self-service report so a caller can pick narrowing / fine-tuning /
lexical-first without running the experiment.

IndexBuildOptions.description:
Optional JSON body for the data-plane index-build routes (`/v1/index/run`
and `/v1/index/build`). Every field is optional: an empty body resolves the
ANN space the way the search path does — a managed embedding config selects
its `stored` space (its dim winning), otherwise the server's hash default —
so a bare reindex always rebuilds the space queries actually look up.
Supplying `provider` overrides that resolution, e.g. to persist a build over
committed BYO vectors with an explicit `stored`/`external_vectors` space.

build_permutation.description: Build the object-storage-native permutation view (PSO). Off by default.

dim.description:
Embedding dimension when no `provider.dim` is given. Defaults to the
server's configured dim. Must match the committed vectors' dimension.

include_raw_rerank.description: Append a raw-f32 rerank region to the embedding run. Off by default.

max_clusters.description: ANN clusters per space. Defaults to the server's configured value.

oneOf.description:
Embedding provider for the ANN family. `None` keeps the server default
(hash). Use `stored`/`external_vectors` to index committed BYO vectors.

IndexDeltaResponse.description:
Outcome of an incremental index-run delta build (`index delta`, or the
commit-path auto-delta trigger). `built` is true when a delta segment was
appended; `folded` is true when the chain was instead rebuilt into a fresh
base (chain too long, or freshness fold). `skipped_reason` explains a no-op
(no base run, nothing un-indexed).

IndexFamilyVisibility.description: Bounded, user-facing summary of one family in the unified visibility manifest.

oneOf.description:
Highest commit folded into the family's merged run. For `merged_only`
families this is compaction position only; query freshness comes from the
family's delta/L0 path plus bounded tail union and snapshot verification.

model_hash.description: Distinguishes ANN model spaces without exposing object keys.

target_hash.description: Distinguishes target-specific BM25/ANN families without exposing object keys.

dry_run.description: Report what would be deleted without deleting anything.

include_branch_shared.description:
Delete runs even when other branches exist in the graph. Child
branches fall back to parent-branch index runs until they build their
own, so deleting shared parent runs can force child rebuilds.

keep_runs.description:
Keep this many newest index runs per family (bm25/ann grouping key).
The newest run is always kept regardless of this value.

reader_grace_commits.description:
Reader grace: retain any run whose snapshot is within this many commits
of the current head, even when it exceeds `keep_runs`. A search that
already resolved a now-superseded run keeps reading its objects, so
collecting a recently-passed run out from under an in-flight reader
surfaces as a NotFound/500. The grace window protects those runs until
the head has advanced far enough that no in-flight search can still be
holding them. Set to 0 to disable the grace and collect purely by
`keep_runs` ordering.

skipped_branch_shared.description:
Set when deletion was skipped because other branches may share this
branch's index runs and `include_branch_shared` was false.

IndexLineage.description: Typed convergence view over the persisted serving families.

manifest_view_token.description:
Content identity of the head generation plus the exact three manifest
views observed while constructing this response.

IndexVisibilityFamily.description: One of the index families published through the unified visibility manifest.

IndexVisibilityFamilyClass.description: How a family participates in the unified visibility watermark.

IndexVisibilityManifest.description: The unified, per-graph visibility view consumed by the console and status tools.

visible_seq.description: Shared published watermark. Lockstep families define this value.

InferenceRule.description:
A single inference rule (SHACL-AF `sh:TripleRule` shape): a BGP `body` (the
condition / `WHERE`) and a `head` triple template instantiated once per
binding. Every variable used in `head` must be bound by `body`.

body.description:
The rule condition: a basic graph pattern over current edges (asserted +
already-derived), joined on shared variables.

combinators.description:
Negation/existence filters folded over the `body` solutions, in order.
Only `exists` (semijoin — keep body rows that have a compatible match) and
`not_exists` (antijoin — keep body rows that have none) are supported in a
rule; `union`/`optional`/`minus` are rejected. A `not_exists` over a
relation produced by another rule's head places this rule in a strictly
higher evaluation stratum (so the negated relation is fully derived first);
a negation cycle is rejected. This is what lets a rule express universal
conditions like "phase complete when no deliverable is incomplete". A
filter pattern may introduce its own local variables and may reference
`body` variables to correlate, but it never binds a `head` variable.

head.description:
The triple template derived once per `body` binding. `subject`/`object`
are variables bound by `body` (or fixed entities); `predicate` is a
relation name.

order.description:
SHACL `sh:order`. Rules run low-to-high; a determinism hint for the
positive (monotone) rules supported today, where the least fixpoint is
order-independent.

InferenceRunRequest.description:
Run a set of inference rules to a bounded fixpoint and return the derived
edges as a **preview**. Derived facts are never written: the write gate
refuses `origin=Derived`, so this surfaces "what follows" without mutating
the graph. Stratified persistence (`derived/v1`) is a separate path.

as_of_commit_seq.description:
Snapshot pin: run inference over the graph as of this `commit_seq`, hiding
later commits. Errors if it exceeds the head.

max_derived.description: Fail-closed ceiling on total derived edges (clamped server-side).

max_rounds.description: Fail-closed ceiling on fixpoint rounds (clamped server-side).

max_solutions.description: Per-rule ceiling on the `body` join's solution set (clamped server-side).

truncated.description:
True if a bound (rounds / derived edges / per-rule solutions) was hit, so
the derived set may be incomplete.

parked.description:
True when the job failed terminally because the provider rejected it for
an account data-policy / configuration reason (a ZDR-ineligible model,
auth, model-not-found) rather than a transient error — a *parked* job that
will not retry. The console renders this as a "choose a different model"
block instead of a retry. It clears when the embedding config version
changes, which mints a fresh reconciliation job.

ManagedEmbeddingBackfillResponse.description: Outcome of embedding the corpus and rebuilding its Stored ANN index.

ManagedEmbeddingConfig.description: The resolved, versioned managed embedding configuration.

ManagedEmbeddingConfigRequest.description: Set the graph branch's default managed embedding model.

dim.description:
Vector width. Required for `modal`; optional for `open_router`, where a
one-text validation call discovers the model's native width.

ManagedEmbeddingConfigResponse.description: Read/set response. Legacy hash-derived graphs return `configured=false` and no config.

oneOf.description:
Durable corpus reconciliation for the current config version. `Active`
is truthful only after this job succeeds.

ManagedEmbeddingModel.description: One model returned by the live managed-embedding service catalog.

output_modalities.description:
The model's output modalities (e.g. `["embeddings"]`). The catalog is
filtered to embedding models on this field, so every entry carries the
embeddings modality; it is surfaced so clients (and the deploy smoke) can
re-verify the catalog is embeddings-only.

policy_eligible.description:
True when the model is eligible under the account's data policy — for the
hosted OpenRouter path, that it has a Zero-Data-Retention route. The
catalog only returns eligible models, so this is `true` for every entry
today; the console can trust it to gate selection if ineligible models are
ever surfaced alongside them.

prompt_price.description: Provider prompt price as a decimal USD/token string when advertised.

ManagedEmbeddingModelsResponse.description: Live embedding models available on this deployment.

ManagedEmbeddingPromoteResponse.description: Response after promoting a finished training run to the graph default.

ManagedEmbeddingPromotionEvals.description: Quality comparison used when promoting a fine-tuned embedding run.

ManagedEmbeddingService.description: Service used to generate managed corpus and query embeddings.

ManagedEmbeddingSource.description: Where a managed embedding model came from.

key.description: Object key of the artifact (under the tenant prefix, so purge covers it).

ModelCadenceResponse.description:
`GET /v1/models/cadence` — the auto-index doubling policy applied to
models: retraining is due when the graph has doubled since the promoted
run was trained (or when nothing was ever trained).

signal_from_seq.description: Signal-bus range the training examples were drawn from (commit seqs).

synthetic_fraction.description: Fraction of examples that were execution-verified synthetic pairs.

ledger_ref.description: Pointer into the quality/perf ledger row that gates promotion.

ModelRegistryResponse.description: `GET /v1/models/registry` — the runs of one kind, newest first.

current.description: The promoted run number, when a `CURRENT` pointer exists.

base_model.description:
The base model this run derives from (e.g. `bge-small-en-v1.5`,
`Qwen3-1.7B`); free-form but stable within a kind.

promoted.description: Advisory at write time; promotion truth is the CAS `CURRENT` pointer.

recipe_id.description:
The training/derivation recipe identifier (searchable; the flywheel
driver hill-climbs recipe space).

oneOf.description:
Reserved post-quantum signature envelope (SLH-DSA; ECC forbidden for
these artifacts). `null` until signing lands — the field exists from
day one so signed and unsigned manifests share one schema.

source_job_id.description:
Durable background job that produced this run. The server assigns this
from the queue's stable job ID so redelivery returns the same run.

ModelRunView.description:
One registry entry as reads report it: the manifest plus its run number
and the *effective* promotion state (from the `CURRENT` pointer, not the
manifest's advisory flag).

ModelServingDefaults.description:
The serving defaults derived from promoted model runs, pinned to the
branch head like the embedding config (`model_defaults_ref`). One object
serves every in-process kind; promotion truth stays the registry's
`CURRENT` pointer — this is its read-optimized projection. **[PIN]**
field names (persisted object).

ModelSplitAudit.description:
`GET /v1/models/split-audit` — makes the WS9 temporal-split obligation
checkable: a trainer MUST train on data ≤ `trained_at_commit_seq` and
evaluate on data > it; the manifest records the lineage window so this
audit can verify it after the fact.

lineage_respects_split.description:
The lineage window closes at or before the split pin
(`signal_to_seq ≤ trained_at_commit_seq`).

split_within_history.description: The split pin lies within the graph's history (`≤ head`).

ModelTrainingConfig.description:
Per-graph automatic-training configuration, stored as one small CAS
object under the branch prefix (purged with the tenant). **[PIN]** field
names. Default: off — training never starts without an explicit opt-in.

auto_train.description: Master switch for cadence-triggered training. Off by default.

kinds.description:
Which [`TRAINABLE_KINDS`] the cadence loop may train. Empty with
`auto_train: true` means "all trainable kinds".

NameSemantics.description:
Whether entity names look like natural language (embeddings will carry) or
opaque identifiers (a hash/lexical approach fits better).

sampled.description: Entity names sampled for the heuristic.

key.description:
Optional stable external key. When set, entity identity is `(type, key)`
and `name` is just a display label — so a later rename keeps the same
entity, and two records that share a name stay distinct by key (e.g. two
"Cambridge" universities in a relational import). Omit it to keep the
default `(type, name)` identity. To attach properties or embeddings to a
keyed entity, set the same `key` on those inputs.

NarrowRelationOp.description:
Remove entity types from a relation's domain and/or range (a subtractive
change). Conflicts when current edges of the relation already use a
removed source/target type — those edges stay (append-only) but begin to
warn. Blocked unless `allow_data_conflicts` is set on the request.

NarrowingRecall.description:
Whether the graph's own entities are retrievable by their names — a proxy
for "will narrowing/retrieval carry". Computed by self-querying a sample and
checking top-10 membership; no LLM.

NeighborhoodEdge.description:
One current-edge projection touching the queried entity, materialized from
the entity's own adjacency slice (peer stub + relation + valid time). Unlike
`StateEntry`/`TripletView` it carries no `previous`/`evidence`/single
`edge_event_id`, because the ranged adjacency run does not store them — so
the ranged and snapshot code paths produce identical edges.

oneOf.description:
The structured form. Required for the fact to be committable — a bare
statement without structure is answered `needs_review` (structuring
free text is the resident extractor's job).

ObserveEpisode.description:
The raw conversation slice to remember. Ground truth — stored verbatim on
the main branch as an `EPISODE` evidence entity.

session_id.description: Caller's conversation id (opaque; drives the default branch name).

turns.description: 1..=500 turns, <= 1 MiB of content in total.

byo_completion.description:
Caller-side extraction: skip the model, run anchoring / supersedure /
gating only.

model.description:
`byo` | `resident`. Defaults to `resident` when the server has a
planner endpoint configured, else `byo`.

anchored.description: Both endpoints resolved to entities that already existed.

supersedes.description: The current edge this fact displaces (commit-ordered reducers only).

auto_merge.description:
Merge the branch back onto the scoped branch when validation is clean
(the WS16 validate-then-merge).

branch.description:
Branch the facts commit to. Defaults to `observe-<hash12(session_id)>`
(branch names are `[a-z0-9-_]{1,32}`, so the session id is hashed, not
embedded). Created from the scoped branch if absent.

extract.description: `false` stores the episode only (no facts, no branch).

branch.description: The branch the facts committed to (empty when `extract: false`).

episode_id.description: Stable entity id of the stored `EPISODE` evidence entity.

idempotent_replay.description: True when this Idempotency-Key was already applied (episode replayed).

snapshot.description: The scoped (main) branch snapshot after the observe.

oneOf.description: SHACL audit of the branch commit (present when shapes are active).

ObserveTurn.description: One conversation turn of an episode.

ts.description: Optional RFC 3339 timestamp of the turn.

OntologyConflict.description:
A current-data conflict found while previewing a subtractive ontology change.
Reported back so the caller can decide whether to re-run with
`allow_data_conflicts` (which keeps the affected records — Little Big Brain never
rewrites or drops them — and applies the change in warn mode).

affected.description: How many current records the change would orphan/flag.

OntologyDefineRequest.description:
Define a custom ontology for the scoped graph before its first commit.
Imports `source` (any [`crate`]-supported format), optionally merges the
built-in default so the standard entity types/relations stay available, and
creates the graph head with the result. Fails if the graph already exists.

format.description: Import format hint. `auto` (the default) sniffs the content.

merge_default.description:
Merge the built-in `default_ai_context_ontology()` underneath the
imported types so the standard AI-context vocabulary stays available.

source.description:
Ontology document text: Little Big Brain JSON, JSON-LD, Turtle/N3, RDF/XML, or
CSV/TSV. Detected from `format` (default `auto`).

OntologyDraft.description: Durable, portable ontology review artifact owned by the graph database.

OntologyDraftCreateRequest.description: Create a reviewable induction artifact without inserting samples into the graph.

OntologyEntityTypeView.description: The full definition of one entity type (class) in the active ontology.

resolvable.description: True when instances of this type can be resolved from free-text mentions.

evidence_ref.description: Stable connector/source reference; sample content is never used as identity.

record.description:
Representative connector record. Objects enable deterministic property
inference; other JSON values remain evidence examples.

OntologyEvolveOp.description:
One additive ontology change for [`OntologyEvolveRequest`]. Additive by
construction — it widens a relation's domain/range or declares a new entity
type, but never narrows or removes — so every record written under an earlier
version stays valid (its positional type/relation ids are preserved). This is
the supported "ontology evolution" path; identity-breaking changes (removals,
narrowing) still require an explicit expand→migrate→contract plan.

OntologyEvolveRequest.description:
Apply a list of additive ontology changes in order, writing a new ontology
version when anything actually changes. Ops are applied against the
in-progress ontology, so an `add_entity_type` earlier in the list is visible
to a later `widen_relation`.

allow_data_conflicts.description:
Allow subtractive ops (narrow/remove) to apply even when current data
conflicts. The affected records are never rewritten or dropped — they stay
readable and simply begin to warn. With this false (the default), a
conflicting subtractive request is rejected without writing and the
conflicts are reported instead.

applied.description:
The changes actually applied (empty on a no-op, or when a conflicting
subtractive request was rejected without `allow_data_conflicts`).

conflicts.description:
Current-data conflicts from subtractive ops. When non-empty and the
request did not set `allow_data_conflicts`, nothing was written and
`ontology_version` is unchanged.

dry_run.description:
True when this response came from `?dry_run=true` and no ontology object
or graph-head update was written.

no_op.description:
Every requested operation was already satisfied, so publishing would not
create a new ontology version.

ontology_version.description:
Active version after a publish, or the predicted resulting version for a
dry run. Equal to `base_ontology_version` for a no-op or blocked preview.

publishable.description:
Whether the exact request can be published. False for a data-conflicting
subtractive preview unless `allow_data_conflicts` was explicitly set.

OntologyInduceRequest.description:
Bounded request for embedding-cluster ontology induction (roadmap item 12):
mine tight embedding clusters among generically-typed entities/edges and
surface each as a **suggested** ontology patch. Suggestions only — nothing
is applied; a human (or the MCP `lbb_configure` `evolve_ontology` action)
feeds the returned `suggested_ops` into
[`OntologyEvolveRequest`](crate::OntologyEvolveRequest) to accept one.

generic_entity_types.description:
Entity-type names treated as "under-typed" (candidates for a more
specific type). Defaults to `["CONCEPT"]` when omitted/empty.

generic_relations.description:
Relation names treated as "under-typed" (candidates for a more specific
relation). Defaults to `["RELATED_TO", "MENTIONS"]` when omitted/empty.

max_clusters.description: Cap on IVF clusters built per population (entities, edges). Default 16.

max_examples.description: Cap on example member ids attached to a suggestion. Default 5.

max_suggestions.description:
Cap on the number of suggestions returned (highest cohesion first).
Default 10.

min_cluster_size.description:
Minimum member count for a cluster to be surfaced as a suggestion.
Default 3.

sample_limit.description:
Cap on how many under-typed entities/edges are pulled into the
clustering pass. Bounded; default 2000, hard max 5000.

seed.description:
Deterministic clustering seed: orders the sampled population before
capping to `sample_limit`, so the same graph + same seed always
clusters the same slice and produces the same suggestions. Default 0.

OntologyPropertyView.description: A typed property on an entity type, as declared in the active ontology.

allowed_values.description:
The field's enforced allowed-value set (`sh:in`), if any — a commit whose
value is outside it is rejected at write time. Empty/absent means the
field accepts any value of its type.

value_type.description:
Lowercase value type: bool | i64 | f64 | datetime | keyword | text |
bytes | vector_f32 | keyword_set | i64_set. The `*_set` types hold a
sorted, deduplicated member set filterable by membership
(`overlaps`/`contains_all`/`not_overlaps`).

OntologyRelationView.description:
The full definition of one relation type (predicate) in the active ontology,
with domain/range resolved to entity-type names so the schema renders without
a second lookup.

dst_types.description: Range — entity-type names this relation can point to (empty = any).

edge_count.description:
Number of current (reduced) edges of this relation in the served snapshot,
when the caller asked for population counts (`GET /v1/ontology?counts=true`).
`0` flags a declared-but-unused relation; `None` (field omitted) means the
count was not requested — the default cheap response skips the snapshot
load. Lets an agent see at a glance which of many declared relations
actually carry edges.

src_types.description: Domain — entity-type names this relation can originate from (empty = any).

exact_types.description:
When false (default), an `entity_types` constraint is satisfied by the
named type *or any subtype* (`rdfs:subClassOf` closure). When true, only
the exact declared type matches — the exact-match escape hatch.

OntologySuggestion.description:
One suggested ontology patch mined from a tight embedding cluster of
currently under-typed (generic) entities or edges. Carries a ready-to-apply
[`OntologyEvolveOp`](crate::OntologyEvolveOp) list so a human reviewer (or
the `lbb_configure` MCP `evolve_ontology` action) can accept it verbatim by
passing `suggested_ops` straight into an
[`OntologyEvolveRequest`](crate::OntologyEvolveRequest) — this engine never
calls `evolve_ontology` itself.

cohesion.description:
Cluster tightness: mean intra-cluster cosine similarity to the
centroid, normalized to `[0,1]` (1 = maximally tight). Suggestions are
returned highest-cohesion first.

example_member_ids.description:
Capped example member ids: `entity:<id>` for an `entity_type`
suggestion, `edge:<id>` for a `relation` suggestion.

inferred_domain.description:
For `kind: relation` — inferred domain (source entity-type names) from
the cluster's edge endpoints. Empty for `kind: entity_type`.

inferred_range.description:
For `kind: relation` — inferred range (target entity-type names).
Empty for `kind: entity_type`.

member_count.description: Total members in the mined cluster (before `max_examples` capping).

proposed_name.description:
Heuristically derived candidate name (e.g. `LIVES_IN`, `WorksAtOrg`),
from the most-frequent meaningful tokens across member labels/evidence.
Purely local lexical heuristics — no external/LLM calls.

source_generic_name.description:
The generic type/relation name this cluster was mined from
(`CONCEPT`, `RELATED_TO`, `MENTIONS`, …).

suggested_ops.description:
Ready-to-apply ops: pass verbatim as `OntologyEvolveRequest.ops` to
accept this suggestion. Never applied by this engine.

OntologySuggestionKind.description: What kind of ontology structure a suggestion proposes.

OntologyTermView.description:
A named entity type or relation in the graph's active ontology. Returned by
the define and read-ontology routes so tenants can confirm the vocabulary in
effect for commits.

concept_count.description: Count of named concepts in the ontology's semantic layer.

decoration_status.description:
Engine-global catalog of which decorations are enforced/advisory/reserved
(see [`decoration_catalog`]). Identical for every graph; lets clients show
honestly what the engine acts on versus merely records.

entity_type_defs.description: Full entity-type definitions (properties, resolvability, version range).

property_defs.description:
The graph's **global** typed-property registry: every scalar field a
commit's `entity_properties` may write, with its declared `value_type`
(`bool`/`i64`/`f64`/`date_time`/`keyword`/`text`/`bytes`) and advisory
`required` flag. Properties are graph-global, not per-type, so
`entity_type_defs[*].properties` is empty — this is the one place to read
"what fields exist and what type is each," the registry a faithful
write→read→rewrite (e.g. a replace-mode migration) needs.

relation_defs.description: Full relation definitions (domain/range, cardinality, inverse, flags).

term_count.description: Count of surface terms (labels/synonyms) in the semantic layer.

PlannerDatasetResponse.description:
`GET /v1/models/planner-dataset` — the planner fine-tune's training feed:
accepted/corrected ask feedback joined to its traces (signals ≤ the split
pin only — the WS9 temporal-split obligation), topped up with
execution-verified synthetic plans so a cold graph can still personalize.

split_seq.description:
Signals with flush seq ≤ this were consumed (record it as the run's
`trained_at_commit_seq` so the split audit can verify).

PlannerExample.description:
One planner training example: a question and the gold structured plan,
plus (for real-feedback rows) the recorded shortlists that rebuild the
exact serving prompt.

shortlists.description:
The `ask_trace` shortlists (`{classes, relations, anchors}`) for
feedback rows; `None` for synthetic rows (the trainer derives
shortlists from the graph vocabulary).

source.description:
`feedback` (accepted/corrected ask) or `synthetic`
(execution-verified, generated from current edges).

PlannerPreferenceDatasetResponse.description:
`GET /v1/models/planner-preference-dataset` — the DPO pass's training
feed: preference pairs joined from `ask_trace` + `ask_feedback` signals
≤ the split pin, topped up with synthetic corrupted-slot pairs.

corrected_count.description: Pairs from `corrected` verdicts (chosen = correction vs decoded plan).

rejected_paired_count.description: Pairs from `rejected` verdicts joined to a same-question accepted plan.

rejected_unpaired.description:
Rejected verdicts left unpaired (no same-question accepted plan yet) —
captured, counted, awaiting a partner.

split_seq.description: Signals with flush seq ≤ this were consumed.

PlannerPreferencePair.description:
One planner preference pair — the DPO pass's training row: the same
question with a plan the user preferred and one they rejected. Corrected
verdicts yield the strongest pairs (chosen = the correction, rejected =
the plan the model actually decoded, same shortlists); plain rejections
pair with a same-question accepted plan when one exists; synthetic pairs
corrupt one slot of an execution-verified gold plan.

original_absent.description:
True when the corrected ask produced no original structured plan. Such
a row is retained as an explicit absent-original preference pair.

shortlists.description: The `ask_trace` shortlists for feedback pairs; `None` for synthetic.

PlannerServingDefaults.description:
Planner serving default derived from a promoted `planner` run: the LoRA
adapter (a path on the model-serving volume) `/v1/ask` passes to the
resident planner endpoint per generate call.

adapter.description: Adapter path on the serving volume (the run manifest's artifact key).

run.description: The promoted registry run this adapter came from.

PlattCalibration.description:
Inline Platt calibration `(a, b)`: maps a raw retrieval score `s` to
`P(relevant) = 1/(1 + exp(a·s + b))`. The server pins it to the snapshot it
serves, so the emitted confidence is reproducible per snapshot
(FAL-DERIVED-DETERMINISM). Fit offline with `lbb-retrieval`'s `PlattScaler`.

PropertyInput.description: One literal property: an ontology field name plus a typed value.

PropertyMergeMode.description:
Whether an `entity_properties` write merges with or replaces the entity's
already-stored fields. See [`TripletCommitFile::property_merge`]. The default
is `Merge`: an entity's attributes accumulate across commits, the
least-surprising semantics for incremental enrichment. `Replace` is the
deliberate, destructive mode for re-stating or pruning an entity's full set.

PropertyValueInput.description:
A typed literal value for ingest. The variant must match the field's declared
