`PropertyType`; `date_time` is an ISO-8601 string stored as microseconds.

oneOf.description:
Members of a `keyword_set` field. Order and duplicates don't matter on
the wire; the commit path canonicalizes (sorts, deduplicates) at rest.

keyword_set.description:
Members of a `keyword_set` field. Order and duplicates don't matter on
the wire; the commit path canonicalizes (sorts, deduplicates) at rest.

oneOf.description: Members of an `i64_set` field; canonicalized like `keyword_set`.

i64_set.description: Members of an `i64_set` field; canonicalized like `keyword_set`.

ProvenanceLeafView.description:
One leaf of a derived edge's provenance trail. A tagged union so a calibrated
retrieval match can never be mistaken for an asserted fact
(FAL-PROV-NOT-LAUNDERED): the two variants are distinct on the wire, and only
the rule engine emits `Asserted` while only the retrieval-premise producer
emits `Retrieval`.

oneOf.description: An asserted edge event this fact transitively rests on (hex id).

A calibrated retrieval match that *seeded* this fact — never an asserted
leaf. Carries the embedding space it came from, the matched entity, and
the calibrated `P(relevant)` used as the premise confidence.

calibrated_score.description: Calibrated `P(relevant)` used as the premise confidence.

RangedReadStats.description:
Read accounting for ranged (block-level) persisted index runs. Reported
when search served from a ranged layout instead of coarse whole objects.

attribute_blocks_fetched.description: Attribute blocks fetched to evaluate filter pruning.

block_bytes_fetched.description: Bytes fetched across all data and attribute blocks.

blocks_fetched.description:
Data blocks fetched with range reads (ANN cluster blocks; BM25
postings plus doc blocks).

blocks_skipped_by_attributes.description:
Blocks skipped thanks to attribute pruning (ANN clusters dropped from
the probe set; BM25 doc blocks never fetched).

candidates_pruned_by_attributes.description:
Candidate entries/postings dropped by attribute pruning before
scoring.

rerank_raw_bytes_fetched.description:
Bytes fetched from raw-f32 cluster regions to exactly rerank vector
candidates. Zero unless exact rerank was requested and the run carries
a raw region.

coalesced_range_gets.description:
Backend ranged GETs saved by P27 coalescing: each value is one ranged
read that pre-warmed several byte-contiguous slices at once.

fetched_byte_budget.description:
Cumulative-fetched-bytes budget (P27); 0 = disabled. Distinct from
`block_byte_budget`, which caps *resident* decoded bytes.

tail_commits_merged.description:
Commits past the indexed run that were merged from the WAL tail
(append-safe edges only; anything else falls back to the snapshot
scan).

data.description:
RDF serialization (Turtle, N-Triples, TriG, or N-Quads) encoded as a JSON
string so the preview metadata stays in one typed response.

RegionAnchorInput.description:
A page-region provenance anchor on a fact's evidence (region provenance
§4.1/§6.4(b) BYO import): where the evidence appears in the source
document. Coordinates are **normalized to the page box** (`[0, 1]`, origin
top-left, y down) with `x0 <= x1` and `y0 <= y1`; `confidence` is the
extraction confidence in `[0, 1]`. The commit path validates and quantizes
(`round(v * 65535)` coords, `round(c * 255)` confidence) into the canonical
stored anchor; out-of-range, non-finite, or inverted boxes are rejected.

page.description: 0-based page index within the source document.

RelationAdjacencyCount.description:
Exact current-edge count for one observed source-type / relation /
target-type combination. The graph summary already scans the reduced edge
set, so exposing this rollup lets schema visualizations avoid one additional
whole-snapshot query per populated relation.

RemoveEntityTypeOp.description:
Tombstone an entity type: existing records of the type stay readable
(the def is retained, so they still resolve), but it is rejected for new
commits. Conflicts when current entities of the type exist; blocked unless
`allow_data_conflicts` is set.

RemoveRelationOp.description:
Tombstone a relation: existing edges stay readable but it is rejected for
new commits. Conflicts when current edges use it; blocked unless
`allow_data_conflicts` is set.

RenameEntityTypeOp.description:
Rename an entity type's display name. The frozen cross-version stable id
is unchanged, so every existing record keeps resolving (now to the new
name) and entity identity is preserved — only the display name and
future commits-by-name change. Errors if `to` already names a different
type. A no-op if the type is already named `to`.

RenameRelationOp.description:
Rename a relation's display name (stable id frozen; existing edges keep
resolving to the new name). Errors on a collision with a different
relation; a no-op if already named `to`.

ResolveTermRequest.description:
WS11 — snap-back resolve: map a piece of free text (a model's guess at a
class, relation, property, or value) to the nearest item that actually
exists in the graph's vocabulary. The output is a subset of real vocabulary
by construction — the resolver never fabricates a term.

candidates.description:
Restrict resolution to this explicit candidate set (the narrowed
shortlist — e.g. relations WS10 admitted for a type pair). Each candidate
is classified against the ontology for its `kind`. When absent the
resolver draws candidates from the graph's schema vocabulary.

kinds.description:
Which vocabulary sources to consider; empty means all. Ignored when
`candidates` is supplied (the candidate list is the search space).

text.description: The free text to snap to real vocabulary (1..=256 chars).

top_k.description: Max matches to return (default 3, clamped to 25).

method.description:
How the matches were ranked: `embedding` (the graph's managed model,
semantic) or `lexical` (trigram/edit-distance, the always-available
fallback used when no managed embedding is configured).

description.description:
Ontology description/definition when the resolved schema term carries
one. Candidate-free class resolution preserves tenant-authored class
documentation instead of returning only a bare label.

score.description: Similarity to the input in [0, 1]; higher is closer.

oneOf.description:
Structured schema signature for class, relation, and property
candidates. This is derived from the same pinned ontology as the
candidate and provenance.

RetractEdgeInput.description: One edge to retract, identified the same way it was asserted.

RetrievalPremiseRequest.description:
Stage G — turn calibrated retrieval candidates into derived edges. Every
candidate scored `P >= threshold` becomes a derived edge `(anchor, relation,
matched)` with `confidence = P` and a single `Retrieval` provenance leaf
(never `Asserted` — FAL-PROV-NOT-LAUNDERED). Candidates come from any
retrieval channel; the server re-verifies each against the served snapshot.

oneOf.description:
The Platt curve mapping raw score → `P(relevant)`. Optional since the
calibration trainer landed: when omitted, the graph's promoted
`calibration` run serves (the response stamps `calibration_run`);
with neither, the request is refused — a made-up curve is worse than
an explicit error.

candidates.description:
Explicit candidates (client ran retrieval). Exactly one of `candidates` or
`query` must be supplied.

materialize.description:
Persist the derived premises as an immutable `derived/v1` object (separate
from the asserted graph) and return its key in `materialized_key`. Default
false — a plain preview that writes nothing.

model_id.description: The embedding model the scores came from (names the `Retrieval` leaf).

query.description:
Query-driven mode: when set, the server runs BM25 entity retrieval for this
text against the served snapshot and uses the matches (with their raw BM25
scores) as candidates — so a caller can hand a query instead of pre-scoring.

relation.description: Predicate of every derived edge; must resolve in the ontology.

target_kind.description: The retrieval target the scores ranked.

threshold.description: Minimum calibrated `P` for a candidate to become a premise (in `[0,1]`).

RetrievalPremiseResponse.description: The derived edges plus the bound / candidate-not-final instrumentation.

calibration_run.description:
The promoted `calibration` run whose scaler priced these premises;
absent when the request carried an inline `calibration`.

considered.description: Candidates examined (`max_premises` may stop the scan early).

dropped_not_visible.description: Candidates dropped because they were not visible in the served snapshot.

materialized_key.description:
Object key of the persisted `derived/v1` run when `materialize` was set;
`None` for a plain preview.

truncated.description: True if the `max_premises` bound stopped emission early.

RetrievalProfileId.description:
Named retrieval profile selecting the fusion knobs (BM25 normalization,
channel weights, score floor, degree-boost mode, ontology class-intent +
expansion de-noise). A closed enum — the only profile identifier that may
reach a metric label — so the cardinality stays bounded.

- `ndcg_v1` is the **shipped default** (a search that names no profile
  resolves to it): `graph_aware_v1` plus candidate-scoped lexical scoring
  and a moderate vector lift, selected by the 2026-06-22 nDCG@10 gate.
- `graph_aware_v1` is the graph-aware comparison anchor: ontology class-intent
  surfacing + entity-fold expansion de-noise, ≈2.5× nDCG@10 over the legacy
  blend on membership/dimension queries, generalising cross-corpus with the
  same knobs and inert on graphs without ontology class structure
  (retrieval-quality-ledger R21/R23).
- `baseline` is the historical hardcoded blend — kept selectable as the
  comparison anchor and the instant rollback path.
- `scored_atom_v1` flips BM25 to the candidate-set-independent theoretical-max
  normalizer (`S_max`): quality-neutral on a single lexical channel, better
  cross-channel fusion calibration (retrieval-quality-ledger R15/R16).

RowPage.description:
Final-result row page metadata. `total` is the number of rows after filters,
projection/deduplication, grouping, and ordering but before the requested
response page is sliced. `returned` is the number of rows actually present in
this response page.

RuleSet.description:
A versioned, immutable set of inference rules stored with a graph branch
(Track B). Pinned from `GraphHead.rules_ref`; used by `infer` and SHACL
`include_derived` when the request carries no inline rules.

version.description: Monotonically increasing; bumped on each define.

RuleSetDefineRequest.description: Define (replace) the stored rule set for a graph branch.

truncated.description:
True when `result_count` is exact but `results` contains only the
caller-requested leading window. Whole-graph audits can produce one row
per offending focus node, so HTTP responses are bounded by default.

source_node_shape.description:
The containing root/node shape that selected the focus node. This stays
actionable when `source_shape` is an inline `sh:property` blank node.

source_shape.description:
The exact SHACL shape that emitted the result. For inline property
constraints this may be a blank node.

oneOf.description:
Live audit health summary for the active shape bundle at the current
snapshot when `/v1/schema?audit=true` is requested. Full result rows
remain on `/v1/schema/audit`.

constraint_shape_count.description:
Internal SHACL shape count including referenced property/logical shapes.
Useful for debugging parser expansion without making `shape_count`
surprising to authors.

shape_count.description:
User-visible active shape count: root SHACL shapes that select focus
nodes, not the inline `sh:property` blank-node constraints.

format.description:
Format hint for this source. Ontology sources use the ontology importer
formats (`auto`, `lbb_json`, `json_ld`, `rdf_xml`, `csv`, `tsv`, or
`spec`). SHACL shapes use RDF graph formats only: `auto`/`turtle`,
`n_triples`, `n_quads`, or `trig`.

ScoredCandidateInput.description:
One scored retrieval candidate on the wire: which entity matched and its raw
(pre-calibration) score from whatever channel produced it. The entity is
referenced by selector (id, or type+name); a candidate not visible in the
served snapshot is dropped, not emitted (inv. candidate-not-final).

aliases.description:
Other names this entity is known by: when SAME_AS-linked entities fold
onto one canonical hit, the folded members' names (and record aliases)
surface here. Absent for entities with no SAME_AS component.

attributes.description:
Requested entity attributes, read from the same loaded snapshot used to
rank this hit. Absent when the request did not ask for a projection.

oneOf.description:
Auditable fusion accounting. Present only when `explain=true`; the
weighted channel contributions plus `post_fusion_adjustment` reproduce
`final_score` within floating-point tolerance.

SearchConsistency.description:
Read consistency for a query surface (search, graph summary, SPARQL).

A5 (2026-07-21 product decision): `Eventual` is the **default**. It serves the
last published index/dataset state at its watermark with no in-memory fold of
the un-indexed tail — the lowest-latency mode and the availability floor
during any indexing backlog. The served watermark rides back on
`SnapshotView::served_at_seq`. `Strong` (fold the un-indexed tail up to the
query head) is now explicit opt-in; the precise read-your-writes contract
(`min_indexed_seq`) replaces most former uses of strong. See
`docs/architecture/segment-native-indexing.md` §6.

oneOf.description:
Co-bind the candidates with a graph anchor's neighborhood (the
engine-native query-conditional join; R17). `None` == no graph constraint.

A5 read-your-writes floor: require the read to be served from state whose
watermark ≥ this `commit_seq`. Take the `new_commit_seq` a commit/import
returned, then read with `min_indexed_seq` set to it. Under the default
`Eventual` consistency, a floor not yet covered by published state returns a
retryable `read_your_writes_pending` 429 (poll until it lands) rather than a
silently stale answer. Under `Strong` the floor is satisfied by construction
(strong covers head); a floor above head is a client error. `None` == no
floor. See `docs/architecture/segment-native-indexing.md` §6.
Named retrieval profile (fusion knobs). `None` == shipped default.

query_vector.description:
Client-computed query vector for the vector signal; when present the
provider only identifies the embedding space.

rerank.description:
Opt into exact raw-f32 reranking of the persisted vector candidates.
Only effective when the run carries a raw-f32 region (built with
`include_raw`); otherwise it is a no-op. Default off keeps the compact
4-bit-approximate path.

rerank_top_k.description:
How many top approximate candidates to re-score exactly before
truncating to the result `top_k`. Defaults to a small multiple of
`top_k` when unset; ignored unless `rerank` is set.

impressions_scanned.description:
Impression objects scanned to build `impressions` (0 unless the request
passed `?include=impressions`).

SearchFeedbackExportImpression.description:
One opt-in-logged search's ranking context, returned in the training export
under `?include=impressions`. The `split` mirrors the query family's labeled
split (or a deterministic hash split when the query has no labels yet), so
negatives mined from `shown` respect the same holdout as the labels.

embedding_model_id.description:
The embedding model in force when the search ran; keeps mined negatives in
the same embedding space as the labels (`None` for an unmanaged graph).

impressions.description:
Present only when the request passed `?include=impressions`. The raw
ranking context of opt-in-logged searches (L1), split-tagged consistently
with the labeled rows so a trainer can mine shown-but-unlabeled entities
as sampled negatives without straddling the train/eval holdout.

target_missing.description:
The judged target no longer exists at the source graph's head (deleted or
never committed). Trainers skip these; a UI can prompt relabeling. Only
computed when the export caller supplies a live-target resolver (the HTTP
endpoint and `lbb-testctl graph feedback-export` both do, from a loaded
snapshot that respects visibility); otherwise it stays `false`. Additive:
`serde(default)` false. Golden-pinned field name.

retracted.description:
A retraction of a prior label for this `(query, target, labeler)`. Sent as
a normal append (`POST /v1/search/feedback` with `retracted: true`) so the
audit trail is preserved — nothing is deleted. The export drops the tuple
when the *latest* event for it is a retraction; a later non-retracted
resubmission revives it. Additive: `serde(default)` false keeps every
pre-retraction stored label decodable. Golden-pinned field name.

untrainable_labels.description:
Accepted labels that cannot currently produce a training-export row.
This is an explicit warning signal: the append-only audit event is kept,
but a trainer will exclude it until that target kind is supported.

SearchFeedbackSummaryResponse.description:
Constant-size administration view over the feedback ledger. Unlike the
qrels export this never returns label or impression rows.

latest_label_sequence.description:
Append-only event count for polling. Exact unless `truncated` is true;
idempotent replays do not advance it and retractions do.

objects_scanned.description:
Label objects fetched for this request. Zero on a materialized-cache hit;
a rebuild reports the bounded source objects it read.

oneOf.description:
Set membership: matches when the field has **any** member equal to one of
`values`. On a scalar field this behaves like `in`. The natural
authorization filter: `{"op":"overlaps","field":"acl","values":[…]}`
admits a document whose ACL set intersects the caller's principals.
`contains_any` is accepted as an alias.

Set membership: matches when the field has a member equal to **every**
one of `values`. On a scalar field this only matches a single-element
`values` equal to the scalar.

Negated `overlaps`: matches when the field has **no** member equal to any
of `values` — including when the field is absent.

post_fusion_adjustment.description:
Type boosts, score floors/clamps, graph-anchor boosts, observation-owner
promotion, and class-intent promotion are accounted for here.

SearchIndexJobStatusResponse.description: Durable background full-index job status with per-family terminal result.

build_adjacency.description: Build the ranged adjacency run for traversal. Off by default.

build_permutation.description:
Build the object-storage-native permutation view (PSO) for native SHACL
target/path evaluation and analytic graph queries. Off by default.

include_raw_rerank.description:
Append a raw-f32 region to the embedding run so vector search can opt
into exact rerank. Off by default (compact 4-bit-only runs).

index_lineage.description:
One coherent observation of every serving index family after this run.
Unlike the per-family build outcomes, this also includes families the
request did not rebuild and is therefore the convergence contract.

SearchOrder.description:
Sort direction for a query's `order_by` key. `Desc` is the default for the
implicit relevance ordering (highest score first); `Asc` is most useful when
ordering by a metadata field.

SearchOrderBy.description:
Deterministic ordering key for a search result page. The default (`Score`)
ranks by relevance; `Metadata` orders by a result's metadata field so a
client can page by, e.g., a timestamp or title. Either way ties break on the
stable document id, so paging with `offset` never skips or repeats a result.

oneOf.description: Order by relevance score (the default ranking).

Order by a string-valued metadata field. Results missing the field sort
last regardless of direction, then fall back to the document-id
tiebreaker, so the page boundary stays deterministic.

SearchSessionCommitRequest.description:
`POST /v1/search/session/commit` — execute the search against the session's
pinned snapshot. The warmed cache makes it read warm; the results are exactly
what the same request would return without any prior `prefix` frames.

pin.description:
Enforce the session's snapshot pin: the search runs with
`as_of_commit_seq` set to the commit the session opened at, so a write
that landed mid-session cannot change what this commit returns
(reproducible runs / retry determinism). An explicit conflicting
`request.as_of_commit_seq` is an `invalid_input`. Default false — the
commit reads the current head, exactly like `POST /v1/graph/search`.

SearchSessionCommitResponse.description:
Commit result: the ordinary search response plus a `speculation` block
reporting how much of the read set the prior warming covered. The `response`
is byte-identical to a non-session search of the same request (equivalence
invariant); only `speculation` reflects that warming happened.

oneOf.description: Present when speculation is enabled: what the warming covered.

SearchSessionOpenResponse.description:
Response to `POST /v1/search/session` — a freshly opened, snapshot-pinned
session. `session_id` is unguessable and scoped to the authenticated tenant.

commit_seq.description: The head commit the session opened at.

session_id.description: Opaque, unguessable session id (hex). Pass it on every `prefix`/`commit`.

snapshot_token.description: The snapshot the session is pinned to, as a consistency token.

ttl_seconds.description: Sliding time-to-live; each frame refreshes it.

SearchSessionPrefixRequest.description:
`POST /v1/search/session/prefix` — the full query prefix typed so far. `seq`
is client-monotonic; a stale `seq` (≤ the last one seen) is a 200 no-op.

seq.description: Client-monotonic frame counter; stale frames are ignored.

text.description: The full prefix so far (not a delta) — the engine diffs it internally.

SearchSessionPrefixResponse.description:
Response to a `prefix` frame: index-grounded suggestions for the prefix plus
a report of what the frame warmed and the session's remaining warm budget.

budget_remaining.description: The session's remaining warm budget after this frame.

stale.description: `true` when this frame's `seq` was stale (≤ the last seen) — a no-op.

suggestions.description: WS1 grounded suggestions for the prefix (real vocabulary, capped).

warmed.description: What this frame warmed into the block cache.

type_boosts.description:
Optional per-entity-type multiplicative rank boost, keyed by entity type
name (e.g. `{"CHUNK": 1.5, "OntologyProposal": 0.0}`). A type absent from
the map is unaffected (multiplier 1.0); `0.0` soft-excludes a type. This
makes heterogeneous-type ranking type-aware — e.g. float document chunks
above ontology-meta entities — without a hard type filter.

SearchSuggestRequest.description:
Turn-level grounding: complete a prefix from the index's own vocabulary
(BM25 term dictionary, attribute directory) and the graph ontology
(classes, relations, properties). Never reads doc/postings blocks; the
completions are strings that provably exist in the graph at this snapshot.

as_of_commit_seq.description:
Transaction-time vocabulary pin. Mutually exclusive with
`snapshot_token`.

oneOf.description:
Type-signature narrowing context. When either endpoint type is set,
`relation` suggestions are filtered and ranked by the graph's empirical
type signatures (the map `relation → {(src_type, dst_type)}` learned from
current edges at this snapshot), falling back to declared domain/range
only when it is narrower. A type pair with no history returns the
unfiltered ranking — narrowing never produces an empty relation set.

field.description: Restrict `attribute_value` suggestions to one attribute field.

kinds.description: Which suggestion sources to draw from; empty means all.

limit.description: Max suggestions to return (default 8, clamped to 50).

oneOf.description:
Explicit ranker-weight override. Precedence per field:
request > trained serving defaults (a promoted `suggest_ranker` run) >
the shipped kind weights. Mirrors `SearchSignalWeights` on search.

snapshot_token.description:
Opaque graph snapshot identity previously returned by LBB. Mutually
exclusive with `as_of_commit_seq`.

Opaque vocabulary token binding graph epoch, commit, and ontology
version. Feed it to a subsequent suggest/resolve request to fail closed
on vocabulary drift.

trained_ranker_run.description:
The promoted `suggest_ranker` run whose trained weights ranked this
response; absent when the shipped defaults (or a request override)
applied.

truncated.description: True when more candidates matched than `limit` returned.

document_frequency.description: Document frequency, when the source carries it (`term`).

signature_forced.description:
True when this is a `relation` suggestion and the request's type-signature
context (`src_type`/`dst_type`) admits exactly one relation — i.e. the type
pair *forces* this predicate. Only ever set under
[`SearchSuggestRequest::context`]; absent (via `skip_serializing_if`)
otherwise, so context-free responses are byte-identical to before WS10.

SearchTemporalCoverage.description:
How a temporally-pinned (or head) search resolved run selection: the
effective `as_of` ceiling, the base snapshot of the selected persisted
run(s), and the highest commit each leg's run+segments actually covered
(the forward WAL overlay covers `(covered_through, ceiling]`). Distinct from
the ontology-inspect `TemporalCoverage` type. Per-leg fields are `None` when
that leg served from an ephemeral rebuild (no persisted run at the ceiling).

oneOf.description:
Highest commit the BM25 run + applicable delta segments cover at the
ceiling; the BM25 overlay covers `(bm25_covered_through, ceiling]`.

Base snapshot commit_seq of the selected BM25 run (`None` if the BM25 leg
was disabled or degraded to an ephemeral rebuild).

ceiling_commit_seq.description:
Resolved transaction-time ceiling: the `as_of_commit_seq` pin, or the
head commit_seq when the query was unpinned.

oneOf.description:
Highest commit the ANN run + applicable delta segments cover at the
ceiling (same semantics as `bm25_covered_through`); the ANN overlay
covers `(vector_covered_through, ceiling]`.

Base snapshot commit_seq of the selected ANN run (`None` if the vector
leg was disabled or degraded to an ephemeral rebuild).

as_of_commit_seq.description:
Snapshot pin (transaction-time ceiling): results hide every event
committed after this sequence, reproducing that past snapshot. BM25/ANN
indexes still generate candidates from the head; the pin is enforced
where results are verified against the snapshot, never by candidate
generation. Omitted means the current head.

as_of_valid_time.description:
Valid-time cursor (RFC 3339): results reflect facts true at this
instant. Omitted means the latest valid time.

query.description:
The natural-language query. The only required field — every other field
has a sensible default, so `{"query":"…"}` is a complete request.

targets.description:
Which result surfaces to return. Omitted or empty means the full default
set (concepts, entities, assertions, paths, observations) rather than a
rejected request — a caller no longer has to guess this field.

suggested_queries.description:
Populated only when the result set is empty: index-grounded
alternatives (vocabulary terms, schema labels) the caller might have
meant. Absent (not `[]`) when there are results or rescue is disabled.

bm25_degraded_to_ephemeral.description:
True when a **persisted** BM25 index was requested but none matched the
query's resolved target set, so the search silently fell back to a full
ephemeral rebuild (much slower). A common cause is building the index for
a narrower target set than the query resolves to (e.g. `[Entity]` vs the
`{Entity, Neighborhood}` that `Entities` resolves to). Also bumps the
`lbb_search_degraded_total{family="bm25"}` metric.

engine_remainder_ms.description:
Difference between `total_ms` and the sum of the named engine phases.
This captures response assembly and other small engine work that is not
worth a dedicated phase row.

oneOf.description:
Graph co-bind (`graph_anchor`) accounting. `mode` is set when a co-bind
ran; `anchor_resolved` is false if the anchor entity wasn't found (the
co-bind is then a no-op); `community_size` is the anchor neighborhood size.

The effective retrieval profile this search ran under (after the server
folds request > per-stack > server-default precedence into the request).
`None` == `baseline`. Lets a client (e.g. the console A/B view) label which
profile produced a result set.

target_prep_ms.description:
Time spent preparing the concrete search target set, optional filtered
target allow-list, and snapshot-visible target join set before BM25/vector
candidate generation.

oneOf.description:
Which persisted runs served this search relative to the resolved `as_of`
ceiling (BM25 and vector legs). Present only when `explain` is set. See
[`SearchTemporalCoverage`].

trained_fusion_run.description:
The promoted `fusion` model run whose trained weights served as this
search's channel-weight defaults (WS9 trainer loop). `None` when the
graph has no promoted fusion run or the request pinned an explicit
profile — so a client can label a ranking "trained (run N)" vs
"profile defaults" honestly.

vector_channel_skipped.description:
True when latest-head hybrid search requested the persisted vector/ANN
channel but no matching run existed, so the optional vector channel was
skipped instead of silently rebuilding an O(corpus) ephemeral ANN on the
request path. Explicit ephemeral and historical pinned searches retain
their existing behavior.

vector_degraded_to_ephemeral.description: As `bm25_degraded_to_ephemeral`, for the persisted vector/ANN index.

vector_model_id.description:
The embedding model the vector leg actually searched: `Some(model_id)` for
a real-embedding space (a graph's managed `Stored` model or client-supplied
`ExternalVectors`), `None` for the char-trigram hash space (or when the
vector leg was disabled). Lets a client label the vector row honestly —
"real embeddings · {model}" vs "auto-derived from text (hash)".

as_of_commit_seq.description:
Snapshot pin (transaction-time ceiling): seeds and expansion reproduce
the graph as of this commit sequence. Any as-of (valid time or pin)
routes traversal through the snapshot scan — the persisted-vector
ranged fast path serves head reads only. Omitted means head.

fields.description:
Entity properties to project for every returned seed and path node.
Projections are reduced from the same snapshot used for authorization
and traversal. Requesting fields routes the operation through the exact
snapshot path rather than ranged adjacency.

max_block_bytes.description:
Decoded adjacency-block working-set byte budget for persisted-vector
ranged expansion. Defaults to the server/store budget. A request may
lower the budget, but not raise it.

projected_nodes.description:
Requested properties keyed by entity id for every returned seed and
path node. Empty when `fields` was omitted.

oneOf.description:
Present when both seed resolution and expansion ran against persisted
ranged indexes (no snapshot load); `None` on the snapshot path.

SetPropertyConstraintOp.description:
Set (or clear) the enforced allowed-value set on an **existing** property
field — the tightening path for a field already in use (e.g. constrain
`status` to its ADR vocabulary). A non-empty list installs the `sh:in`
constraint that future commits are checked against; an empty list clears
it. Enforcement is write-time only: values already stored are not
retroactively validated. Errors on an unknown field or a non-enumerable
type. Bumps the ontology version.

allowed_values.description: Canonical allowed values; empty clears the constraint.

property.description: Name of the existing property field to constrain.

SetRelationCardinalityOp.description:
Change a relation's cardinality (`one_to_one` | `one_to_many` |
`many_to_one` | `many_to_many`). Advisory metadata (it informs ranking and
query shaping, and is not enforced against stored edges), so it never
conflicts with existing data.

SetRelationInverseOp.description:
Set (or replace) a relation's inverse-relation display name, enabling
one-hop reverse traversal (e.g. `PHASE_OF` for `HAS_PHASE`). Metadata
only — never touches stored edges.

ShaclDatatype.description:
SHACL `sh:datatype` family, matched against a stored [`PropertyValue`] kind.
`decimal` accepts any numeric (integer or float); the rest are exact.

ShaclLogical.description:
SHACL logical shape combinators over [`ShaclShapeConstraints`] operands, all
applied to the same focus node. Empty lists impose no constraint.

and.description: `sh:and`: the node must conform to **every** operand.

not.description: `sh:not`: the node must conform to **none** of these operands.

or.description: `sh:or`: the node must conform to **at least one** operand.

xone.description: `sh:xone`: the node must conform to **exactly one** operand.

closed.description:
`sh:closed`: when true, a focus node may carry **only** the outgoing
relations and literal fields named by this shape's property `path`s (plus
`ignored_properties`). Any undeclared one emits a `closed` violation.
`validate` mode only.

deactivated.description:
`sh:deactivated`: when true the entire shape is skipped — no focus nodes,
no matches, no violations.

oneOf.description:
Logical shape combinators (SHACL `sh:and` / `sh:or` / `sh:not` /
`sh:xone`) applied to each focus node. A failing combinator emits one
node-level `ValidationResult` (`and`/`or`/`not`/`xone`); each combinator
is independent. `validate` mode (and `select` gating).

target_objects_of.description:
`sh:targetObjectsOf`: focus on every entity that is the **object**
(destination) of at least one relation of this name.

target_subjects_of.description:
`sh:targetSubjectsOf`: focus on every entity that is the **subject**
(source) of at least one relation of this name.

unique.description:
Cross-focus-node uniqueness constraints: the value reached at each
`path` must be held by at most one focus node of this shape. Only
evaluated in `validate` mode (uniqueness is an aggregate over the
focus set, which the per-node property constraints cannot express).

ShaclPath.description:
A SHACL property path expression, evaluated as a bounded graph traversal from
the focus node to a set of value-node entities. Mirrors SHACL's path grammar:
a single predicate, an inverse, a sequence, an alternative, and the
`*`/`+`/`?` closures.

oneOf.description: A single relation, followed forward (focus -> value).

predicate.description: A single relation, followed forward (focus -> value).

oneOf.description: Follow the inner path backwards.
Follow each sub-path in order (`p1 / p2 / …`).

sequence.description: Follow each sub-path in order (`p1 / p2 / …`).

oneOf.description: The union of the sub-paths (`p1 | p2 | …`).

alternative.description: The union of the sub-paths (`p1 | p2 | …`).

oneOf.description: Reflexive transitive closure (`p*`), bounded.
Transitive closure (`p+`), bounded.
Zero or one step (`p?`).

Literal value constraints (SHACL `sh:datatype` / `sh:minInclusive` /
`sh:maxInclusive` / `sh:minExclusive` / `sh:maxExclusive` /
`sh:minLength` / `sh:maxLength` / `sh:in`). When any of these is set —
or when `path` names a property field rather than a relation — the shape
is evaluated against the focus node's own literal property values for the
`path` field instead of relation targets. `min_count`/`max_count` then
count those literal values. `validate` mode only.

deactivated.description: `sh:deactivated`: when true this property constraint is skipped.

in.description:
Enumerated allowed values (`sh:in`). On a literal (field) path each value
must equal one of these under typed comparison; on a relation path the
value entity must be one of the listed `Entity` selectors.

message.description:
Author-set message overriding the engine-generated one (SHACL
`sh:message`). Applies to every violation of this property shape.

min_length.description: String length bounds (character count); apply to `keyword`/`text` values.

oneOf.description:
Structured SHACL property path. When set it takes precedence over the
single-predicate `path`/`direction` (which then serves only as the
`resultPath` label). The path is evaluated as a bounded graph traversal
reaching a set of value-node entities; entity-value constraints (`class`,
`hasValue`, `min_count`/`max_count`, entity `in`) apply to that set.

pattern.description:
`sh:pattern`: a regular expression the string value must match (applies to
`keyword`/`text` values on a literal path).

oneOf.description:
Author-set severity for this shape's violations (SHACL `sh:severity`).
Absent defaults to `violation`. `validate` mode only.

result_count.description: Matches in `select` mode; violations in `validate` mode.

as_of_commit_seq.description:
Snapshot pin (transaction-time ceiling): shapes evaluate over the graph
as of this commit sequence, hiding later events. Omitted means head.

include_derived.description:
Run `rules` and include the derived edges in evaluation. Default `false`,
so SHACL is **asserted-only** unless explicitly opted in — a rule can't
silently mask a real data-quality violation.

mode.description:
`select` (the default) returns the focus nodes that match each shape.
`validate` instead returns a conformance report listing the focus nodes
that *violate* the shapes' constraints. Validation is detection, not
enforcement: it surfaces duplicates/violations that already exist but
blocks no write.

rules.description:
Inference rules whose derived edges are folded into the snapshot the
shapes evaluate over, **only** when `include_derived` is set. Lets a shape
validate (or select against) inferred facts as well as asserted ones.

oneOf.description: Present only for `validate` mode; `results` is empty in that case.

ShaclShapeConstraints.description:
A reusable bundle of constraints applied to a focus node — the operand of a
logical combinator. Carries property constraints and (recursively) its own
nested logical combinators, but no target (operands share the parent shape's
focus node).

oneOf.description:
Nested logical combinators. Boxed and excluded from schema recursion to
keep OpenAPI generation finite.

message.description:
Optional message attached to each uniqueness violation; a default is
generated when absent.

path.description: Relation whose reached value identifies the focus node (e.g. `HAS_EMAIL`).

ShaclValidationReport.description:
`validate`-mode conformance report. Mirrors the shape of a SHACL
`ValidationReport`: `conforms` is true iff `results` is empty.

ShaclViolation.description: A single conformance failure, mirroring a SHACL `ValidationResult`.

focus.description: The focus node that violated a constraint (SHACL `focusNode`).

path.description:
Relation path the constraint applies to; absent for node-level
constraints (SHACL `resultPath`).

value.description:
Offending or related value nodes: wrong-class values for `class`, the
required target for `has_value`, or the other focus nodes a `unique`
collision is shared with.

hit_rate_at_k.description: Fraction of labeled queries whose expected entity appeared in top-k.

mrr_at_k.description: Mean reciprocal rank of the first expected target in top-k.

recall_at_k.description: Mean fraction of each query's expected targets recovered in top-k.

ShadowEvalRequest.description:
`POST /v1/models/shadow-eval` — champion vs challenger retrieval over the
same pinned snapshot: the challenger is a search configuration (a
challenger embedding run's space, different weights, a profile), replayed
against the same queries the champion serves. Promotion stays a decision
on this evidence — the endpoint never promotes.

as_of_commit_seq.description: Pin both arms to one snapshot for reproducibility.

oneOf.description: Champion arm search options; `None` = the graph's serving defaults.

gate.description:
Fail-closed promotion verdict over the same labeled queries and pinned
snapshot. Requires quality non-regression plus p50/p95/p99 latency
ratios within the configured ceiling; this endpoint still never
performs the promotion itself.

labeled.description: Labeled queries (those with `expected`) the hit rates are over.

ShadowQuery.description:
One shadow-eval probe: a query and (optionally) the entity names that
count as a hit. Pairs come from captured signals, hand-written evals, or
the execution-verified synthetic generator.

overlap_at_k.description: |champion ∩ challenger| / k over the returned top-k names.

SignalEnvelope.description:
The stored form, one JSON object per NDJSON line. **[PIN]** the field set
(`v`, `kind`, `ts`, `request_id`, `snapshot_token`, `payload`) — Wave-5
consumers decode it.

ts.description: RFC 3339 capture timestamp, stamped by the server.

accepted.description: Number of signals written (0 when the feature flag is off).

object_key.description: The object the batch was flushed to, absent when nothing was written.

SignalInput.description:
One signal as a client sends it. The server stamps `v`, `ts`, and (when the
client omits it) `snapshot_token` to produce the stored [`SignalEnvelope`].

SignalKind.description:
The closed set of signal kinds (v1). Extending this is a deliberate format
change, not an open string.

v1.1 (2026-07-07, planner-feedback capture): adds `ask_trace`
(server-emitted per `/v1/ask`: the question, the decoded plan + its
shortlists, verification flags, retrieval outcome) and `ask_feedback`
(client verdict on an ask, joined to its trace by `request_id`) — the raw
material the planner fine-tune trains on.

SignalReadResponse.description:
`GET /v1/signals?from=&to=&limit=` — the consumer side of the signal bus:
signals whose flush seq lies in `[from, to]`, oldest first. Trainers split
on a pinned seq T (train ≤ T, eval > T) and record the window in the WS9
manifest's `data_lineage`.

objects_read.description: Objects fetched to serve this read (cost transparency).

truncated.description: True when the limit cut the result short.

SignatureSparsity.description:
How constrained the graph's relation type-signatures are. Sparse signatures
(many type pairs admitting exactly one relation) mean type-context alone
forces the predicate — the strongest, cheapest grounding.

forced_pct.description:
Fraction of observed (src_type, dst_type) pairs that admit exactly one
relation.

le3_pct.description: Fraction of observed type pairs that admit at most three relations.

type_pairs.description: Distinct type pairs observed in the current edge projection.

oneOf.description:
When a read was pinned with `as_of_commit_seq`, the pin that was applied.
This makes a pinned response self-describing — `commit_seq` still reports
the loaded head, so a caller can see both "you asked for N" and "head is
at M" instead of silently getting head.

compacted_seq.description:
Highest commit folded into the **compacted graph snapshot base** (the
materialized segment base). Commits in `(compacted_seq, commit_seq]` live in
the WAL tail and are replayed on read — so `compacted_seq: 0` is the normal
state of a small or freshly-created graph (no base built yet) and does
**not** mean the data is unreadable: every read replays the full tail.

This is *not* search-index coverage. Whether BM25/vector search indexes
are built — what `/v1/index/run` advances — is reported separately as
`bm25_indexed_commit_seq` / `ann_indexed_commit_seq` on
`GET /v1/graph/metadata` and on the entity read's metadata.

oneOf.description:
The coherent watermark this response was actually served through when it
trails graph head. Usually this is the published index/dataset watermark.
Explorer's adjacency-backed reads report the newest published adjacency
base+delta coverage without inspecting the WAL tail. For a multi-family
search this is the MIN of the enabled families' published watermarks, so
every surface (BM25, vector, …) agrees on one snapshot. Omitted when the
response is served at `commit_seq`.

stale.description:
F2 (roadmap 18.3): true when this snapshot was served from the in-memory
cache while storage was degraded and the latest head could not be
verified — so `commit_seq` may not be the current head. Absent (false) on
every normal read; writes never serve stale. Omitted from the wire form
when false so healthy responses are byte-identical to before.

stale_reason.description:
The reason a read is `stale`. `"storage_degraded"` (F2),
`"eventual_consistency"` (A3: served from the last published index/dataset
state), or `"adjacency_coverage"` (served from the newest published
ranged-adjacency snapshot); omitted when not stale.

SparqlAggregate.description:
One aggregate projection: `func(operand) AS as_var`. `operand` is absent for
`COUNT(*)` (count rows); for SUM/AVG/MIN/MAX it must resolve to a numeric
value (typically a `property` term). `distinct` applies to COUNT.

SparqlAggregateFunc.description: An aggregate function for a grouped SELECT.

SparqlCompareOp.description: A comparison operator for a SPARQL FILTER.

SparqlEntailment.description:
RDFS entailment regime applied when projecting the graph for a conformant
SPARQL query. Controls whether a class term matches subtype instances.

SparqlFilter.description:
A FILTER over a solution: a comparison, or a boolean composition of filters.
A comparison whose operands are unbound, incomparable, or a type mismatch is
not satisfied (treated as false).

SparqlGroup.description:
One result group of a grouped/aggregated SELECT: the GROUP BY key entities
(`keys`, identity keys) and typed scalar keys (`value_keys`, property /
date-bucket keys), plus each aggregate's value bound to its `as` variable.

keys.description: Entity-identity GROUP BY keys, under their variable name.

value_keys.description:
Typed scalar GROUP BY keys (from `group_keys` property/date-bucket
entries), each under its `as` name. Empty for a purely entity-keyed group.

SparqlGroupKey.description:
A GROUP BY key. `var` groups by the identity of the entity bound to a
variable (the legacy `group_by` behavior); `property` groups by a typed
scalar `field` read off that entity, projected into each group's
`value_keys` under `as`. Externally tagged
(`{"var": {…}}`, `{"property": {…}}`).

oneOf.description:
Group by the entity bound to `var` (identity). The group's `keys` map
carries the key entity under `var`, exactly like a `group_by` entry.

var.description:
Group by the entity bound to `var` (identity). The group's `keys` map
carries the key entity under `var`, exactly like a `group_by` entry.

oneOf.description:
Group by the typed scalar property `field` of the entity bound to `var`.
The value lands in each group's `value_keys` under `as`. Rows whose entity
lacks the property form a single `null`-valued group.

property.description:
Group by the typed scalar property `field` of the entity bound to `var`.
The value lands in each group's `value_keys` under `as`. Rows whose entity
lacks the property form a single `null`-valued group.

oneOf.description:
Group by a calendar truncation of a **datetime** property `field` of the
entity bound to `var` — the unit for a time series (commits per month,
events per day). The bucket label (e.g. `2026-06`, `2026-06-25`) lands in
each group's `value_keys` under `as`. Errors if `field` is not a datetime
property. Rows whose entity lacks the property form a `null`-valued group.

date_bucket.description:
Group by a calendar truncation of a **datetime** property `field` of the
entity bound to `var` — the unit for a time series (commits per month,
events per day). The bucket label (e.g. `2026-06`, `2026-06-25`) lands in
each group's `value_keys` under `as`. Errors if `field` is not a datetime
property. Rows whose entity lacks the property form a `null`-valued group.

SparqlKeyValue.description:
One typed GROUP BY key value projected into a group's `value_keys`: a typed
scalar (a property value, or a date-bucket label). Distinct from
[`SparqlScalar`] (numeric-only aggregate results) because a key can be a
string/keyword, datetime, or boolean. `null` is an unbound key (the entity
lacked the property).

oneOf.description: RFC3339 instant (a datetime property) or a bucket label (`2026-06`, etc.).

date_time.description: RFC3339 instant (a datetime property) or a bucket label (`2026-06`, etc.).

SparqlOrderBy.description:
One ORDER BY key. `var` names an aggregate `as` var or a GROUP BY key in
aggregated mode, or a bound variable otherwise; rows/groups are ordered by
that value (numeric for aggregates, entity name for entity keys).

SparqlScalar.description:
A scalar aggregate result value. `null` is an empty AVG/MIN/MAX (no numeric
rows).

SparqlSelectRequest.description:
A SPARQL-subset SELECT/ASK query: a conjunctive basic graph pattern (the
WHERE) evaluated over the object-storage permutation view, with variable
projection, DISTINCT, and LIMIT/OFFSET. Structured-request-first — the SPARQL
text syntax is a later addition behind a dialect field, and FILTER plus
aggregation arrive in later milestones.

aggregates.description:
Aggregate projections (COUNT/SUM/AVG/MIN/MAX … AS …). Presence switches
the query into grouped/aggregated mode.

as_of_commit_seq.description:
Snapshot pin: evaluate as of this `commit_seq`, hiding later commits.
Errors if it exceeds the head; forces the snapshot reduce path.

ask.description:
`ASK` form: return only `boolean` (whether the pattern has any solution).
Projection, DISTINCT, and limit/offset are ignored.

oneOf.description:
Read consistency (A3 serving; A5 default). `eventual` (the **default**
since A5) serves ENTIRELY from the last published conformant prepared
dataset (the maintenance-owned, asserted-only default family, discovered
via the W9c pointer) at its watermark — no in-memory gap fold, so a query
stays available even while background indexing lags the snapshot. The
served watermark is surfaced as `snapshot.served_at_seq`. Eventual serving
requires a published dataset; a graph with none yet returns a retryable
429. `strong` (explicit opt-in) evaluates against the head snapshot,
folding any un-indexed WAL tail into the conformant dataset in memory.

filters.description:
FILTER: boolean conditions on each solution, implicitly AND-ed. A solution
is kept only when every filter holds.

group_by.description:
GROUP BY: variables whose bound entities define the groups. When set (or
when `aggregates` is non-empty) the response returns `groups` instead of
`solutions`. Empty `group_by` with `aggregates` set yields one group over
the whole result.

group_keys.description:
GROUP BY keys richer than a bare entity variable: group by a typed scalar
`property` value (e.g. a commit's `area`), or a `date_bucket` truncation
of a datetime property (e.g. commits per month). Additive to `group_by`;
the effective grouping is the `group_by` variables (as entity-identity
keys) followed by these. Entity-identity keys land in each group's `keys`;
scalar keys land in `value_keys` under their `as` name. Presence (like a
non-empty `group_by` or `aggregates`) switches the query into grouped mode.

having.description:
HAVING: conditions on the aggregated groups, implicitly AND-ed. Only
meaningful in aggregated mode; a `var` term resolves to an aggregate value
or a GROUP BY key entity.

limit.description: Return at most this many rows (applied after `offset`).

max_solutions.description:
Fail-closed ceiling on the intermediate join solution set
(see `AnalyticQueryRequest::max_solutions`).

oneOf.description:
A5 read-your-writes floor: require the read to be served from state whose
watermark ≥ this `commit_seq` (the `new_commit_seq` a commit/import
returned). Under the default `eventual`, a floor not yet covered by the
published dataset returns a retryable `read_your_writes_pending` 429 (poll
until it lands); under `strong` the floor is satisfied by construction and a
floor above head is a client error. `None` == no floor. See
`docs/architecture/segment-native-indexing.md` §6.

order_by.description:
ORDER BY: sort keys applied to the result rows (or groups), in priority
order, before LIMIT/OFFSET.

patterns.description: WHERE: the conjunctive basic graph pattern (shares the analytic engine).

reason.description:
Reason over the graph's stored inference rules: fold the rule-derived
facts into the snapshot the query sees, so a pattern matches asserted **and**
derived edges in one shot. Off by default (asserted-only). Forces the
overlay reduce path (the prebuilt index holds no derived facts), and is a
no-op when the branch has no stored rules.

select.description:
SELECT projection: the variables to return, in order. Absent = `SELECT *`
(every variable the pattern binds, sorted). Ignored in aggregated mode.

SparqlSelectResponse.description:
Result of a `SparqlSelectRequest`. For SELECT, `vars` is the projected head
and `solutions` the rows; for ASK, `boolean` is set and `solutions` empty.

boolean.description: Set for the ASK form: whether the WHERE pattern has any solution.

groups.description: Aggregated/grouped result rows. Empty unless the query aggregates.

solutions.description: Non-aggregated SELECT rows. Empty in aggregated mode and for ASK.

truncated.description:
The intermediate-solution ceiling cut the join
(see `AnalyticQueryResponse::truncated`).

vars.description:
The projected variable names (the result head). For aggregated queries
this is the GROUP BY keys followed by each aggregate's `as` variable.
Empty for ASK.

SparqlTerm.description: An operand in a FILTER comparison.

oneOf.description: The entity bound to a query variable.

var.description: The entity bound to a query variable.

oneOf.description: A literal property `field` of the entity bound to `var`.

property.description: A literal property `field` of the entity bound to `var`.

oneOf.description: A literal constant.

SparqlTextRequest.description:
Run a SPARQL 1.1 query supplied as **text** (SELECT or ASK) through the
conformant engine over the live graph, returning SPARQL 1.1 Query Results
JSON. Distinct from [`SparqlSelectRequest`] (the structured-request form);
this is the raw-text dialect. CONSTRUCT/DESCRIBE are rejected (they yield an
RDF graph, not a results table).

as_of_commit_seq.description:
Snapshot pin: project the graph as of this `commit_seq` before running the
conformant engine, hiding later commits. Errors if it exceeds the head.

as_of_valid_time.description: RFC3339 valid-time; query the graph as of that instant. `None` = now.

entailment.description:
RDFS entailment regime. `Subclass` (default) makes a query for a class
match instances of its subtypes; `None` matches only the declared type.

limit.description:
Return at most this many result rows. Applied to SELECT bindings after the
conformant engine has produced the complete result document.

offset.description: Skip this many result rows before `limit`.

query.description: The SPARQL query text (SELECT or ASK).

reason.description:
Reason over the branch's stored inference rules: project the rule-derived
facts into the dataset the conformant engine runs over, so a query reasons
over asserted **and** derived edges. Off by default; a no-op when the branch
has no stored rules. Combine with `entailment: subclass` (the default) for
full reasoning — rule-derived facts plus `rdfs:subClassOf` type closure.

SparqlTextResponse.description:
Result of a [`SparqlTextRequest`]: the SPARQL 1.1 Query Results JSON document,
carried verbatim as a string (the engine already serializes it to the
standard `{ "head": …, "results": … }` / `{ "head": …, "boolean": … }`
shape). Clients parse `results` as JSON.

oneOf.description:
A3 (bounded-staleness serving): present when the request selected a
consistency mode, so a text-dialect client can read the served watermark
and staleness the same way structured callers read `snapshot`. Omitted
(absent) for a bare strong-consistency request, so existing responses stay
byte-identical.

SparqlValue.description:
A literal constant operand in a FILTER. Scalars compare by typed value;
`entity` compares by identity against a bound variable.

SpeculationExplain.description:
`explain.speculation`: of the blocks this commit read, how many the prior
`prefix` warming had already fetched (`hit`), how many it warmed but the
commit never read (`wasted`), and the total bytes warming moved.

bytes.description: Total bytes warming moved into the cache.

hit_blocks.description: Warmed blocks the commit actually read (the speculation payoff).

warmed_blocks.description: Blocks the session warmed across all its `prefix` frames.

wasted_blocks.description: Warmed blocks the commit never read (wasted speculative work).

SuggestContext.description:
Endpoint types for [`SearchSuggestRequest::context`]. Both optional; supplying
only one narrows on that side alone (a known source type, unknown target).

SuggestDatasetResponse.description:
`GET /v1/models/suggest-dataset` — the suggest-ranker trainer's probe
feed: `suggestion_adopted` signals (each carries the typed prefix and the
adopted suggestion text) at or below the split pin, topped up with
execution-verified synthetic pairs from the graph's own vocabulary so a
cold graph can still train.

probes.description:
`query` = the typed prefix, `expected` = the adopted (or synthetic
gold) suggestion text.

split_seq.description:
Signals with flush seq ≤ this were consumed (record it as the run's
`trained_at_commit_seq` so the split audit can verify).

SuggestRankerWeights.description:
Per-kind multipliers for the suggest ranking (`score = kind_weight ×
df_factor`): the trainable artifact of the `suggest_ranker` model kind.
`None` leaves that kind on the shipped default weight, so a trained vector
only moves the kinds it learned about.

SuggestServingDefaults.description:
Suggest-ranker serving default derived from a promoted `suggest_ranker`
run: the per-kind weights the suggest path applies when a request does not
override `ranker`.

run.description: The promoted registry run these weights came from.

SuggestedQuery.description:
One zero-result-rescue alternative: a query the caller might have meant,
with the source that produced it.

SyntheticEvalResponse.description:
`GET /v1/models/synthetic-eval` — execution-verified QA pairs generated
FROM the graph: each question is templated from a current edge and its
expected answer IS the executed projection, so labels are verified by
construction, never hallucinated. Deterministic per snapshot.

TemporalCoverage.description:
A self-describing summary of a snapshot's two temporal axes, so an agent can
decide up front which temporal queries are meaningful on this graph.

The product motivation: a graph backfilled in one batch with no per-fact
`valid_time` looks fully populated but cannot be bisected by date — every
`as_of` returns the same snapshot. The two `*_degenerate` flags say so
explicitly rather than letting a caller render N identical daily panels.

as_of_valid_time_degenerate.description:
True when no event carries a `valid_time` bound: an `as_of_valid_time`
query returns all-or-nothing, never a daily progression.

distinct_commit_count.description:
Distinct `commit_seq` values among the events: how many commits the data
arrived in. `1` means a single bulk backfill, so `as_of_commit_seq` can
only distinguish "before" vs "after" that one batch.

earliest_commit_time_micros.description: Transaction-time span (when facts were recorded), in epoch micros.

earliest_valid_time_micros.description: World-time span over events that carry a `valid_time` bound, in micros.

edge_event_count.description: Total append-only edge events in the snapshot overlay.

edges_with_valid_time.description: Events whose `valid_time` carries a bounded start and/or end.

head_commit_seq.description:
Head `commit_seq` — the largest snapshot pin (`as_of_commit_seq`) a read
can request right now.

single_commit.description:
True when every event arrived in a single commit (a one-shot backfill):
there is only one snapshot to pin to, and `as_of` by wall-clock date
cannot bisect the history. For multi-commit graphs, inspect the
`*_commit_time_micros` span to judge whether daily bisection is possible.

valid_time_coverage.description:
`edges_with_valid_time / edge_event_count`, in `[0,1]`. Near zero means
an `as_of_valid_time` query cannot bisect this graph by world-time.

TrainGateReport.description:
The deterministic promotion gate's verdict, reported in full so a held
run is as auditable as a promoted one (null results are data).

effect.description:
Challenger − champion primary quality metric on the held-out eval
slice (nDCG for fusion and retrieval-profile comparisons).

labeled.description: Labeled eval probes the decision is over.

latency_p50_ratio.description: Challenger / champion latency ratios at each reported percentile.

latency_ratio.description:
Legacy decision latency ratio. For configuration shadow evaluation this
is the worst of p50/p95/p99; older trainer kinds may use their primary
latency statistic.

max_latency_ratio.description:
Ceiling on `latency_ratio` — unoptimized metrics are guarded, never
traded away.

min_effect.description: Minimum effect size for promotion (refuses noise).

min_labeled.description: Minimum labeled probes for any promotion.

ndcg_ci95_lower.description:
Deterministic paired-bootstrap 95% interval for challenger − champion
nDCG@10. Promotion requires the lower bound to be at least zero.

reason.description: Human-readable one-liner for the console status surface.

recall_delta.description:
Paired held-out retrieval deltas. When present, all must be
non-negative.

TrainModelJobProgress.description: Bounded in-flight work counters for a durable trainer job.

estimated_duration_ms.description:
Linear estimate from work completed so far. Absent before the first
measured probe or for trainers without fine-grained progress.

heartbeat_micros.description: Worker heartbeat attached to this progress snapshot.

replay_evaluations_completed.description: Expensive search executions or cached-score evaluations completed.

source_probes_completed.description: Distinct input probes prepared at the pinned source snapshot.

TrainModelJobStatusResponse.description:
Durable background trainer status. The terminal `result` carries the full
snapshot lineage, held-out gate evidence, and recorded run number.

TrainModelRequest.description:
`POST /v1/models/train` — run the deterministic trainer once for one
kind: build an execution-verified probe set, search a bounded candidate
space on the train slice, gate challenger vs champion on the held-out
eval slice, record the run in the WS9 registry either way, and promote
only when the gate passes. This is the same tick the auto-train cadence
fires; calling it by hand is always safe (bounded, snapshot-pinned).

force.description: Train even when the cadence says retraining is not yet due.

max_probes.description: Probe-set ceiling (train + eval slices together). Clamped server-side.

probes.description:
Bring-your-own labeled probes (captured signals, hand-written evals).
`None` ⇒ execution-verified synthetic pairs from the graph itself.

source_job_id.description:
Server-assigned durable execution identity. Clients normally omit it;
it is persisted into the model manifest to make worker redelivery safe.

TrainModelResponse.description: Outcome + evidence of one trainer tick.

oneOf.description:
Winning Platt scaler for `kind: calibration` ticks. For calibration,
arm `hit_rate_at_k` carries `1 − Brier` (higher is better) and the
manifest metrics carry the explicit Brier scores.

candidates.description: Candidate configurations evaluated on the train slice.

oneOf.description: Best candidate on the held-out eval slice.
Champion (serving defaults) on the held-out eval slice.

run.description: The recorded registry run (promoted or held); absent when nothing ran.

oneOf.description: Winning candidate weights for `kind: suggest_ranker` ticks.

Winning candidate weights (recorded in the run's artifact), for
`kind: fusion` ticks.

TransitionEntry.description:
One transition in an entity's history of a state relation, with the dwell
time spent at this value before the next transition.

at_from_valid_time.description:
True when `at_micros` came from `valid_time.start`; false when it fell
back to the commit (transaction) time.

at_micros.description:
Effective transition instant (epoch micros): the edge's `valid_time.start`
when set, otherwise its transaction time (`commit_time`). Always present,
so dwell time is computable even for live writes that omitted `valid_time`.

dwell_micros.description:
Micros spent at this value before the next transition. `None` for the
current (last) value, unless its `valid_time.end` is set (then it is the
closed interval `end - at`).

as_of_commit_seq.description:
Snapshot pin: reproduce the graph state as of this `commit_seq`, hiding
any event committed later. Errors if it exceeds the current head, and
forces the snapshot scan (the ranged adjacency fast path declines a
pinned read). Omit for the latest snapshot.

fields.description:
Entity fields projected for every seed/intermediate/terminal node in
`TraverseResponse.projected_nodes`.

oneOf.description:
Authorization/metadata predicate applied before a node is admitted or
expanded. Both endpoints of every traversed edge must satisfy it.

max_block_bytes.description:
Decoded adjacency-block working-set byte budget for the ranged path.
Defaults to the server/store budget. A request may lower the budget,
but not raise it. Ignored on the snapshot path.

max_block_reads.description:
Object-read budget for the ranged adjacency path; defaults to a value
derived from `max_frontier_entities`. Ignored on the snapshot path.

projected_nodes.description:
Same-snapshot field evidence for every node appearing in `paths`, keyed
by stable entity-id hex. Present as an empty map when no fields were
requested.

oneOf.description:
Present when the traversal ran against ranged adjacency blocks; `None`
means the snapshot-scan path served the request.

Edge-level duplicate handling. `append` preserves historical behavior:
every emitted triplet appends a new edge event. `skip_unchanged` makes a
triplet a no-op when an identical current edge already exists, which is
useful for re-runnable backfills. Evidence-only repeats are also skipped:
use `append` when a repeated current edge carries new provenance that
must be recorded. Omitted = `append`.

entity_embeddings.description:
Client-supplied vectors to attach to entities in this commit. Entities
are matched by (type, name); ones not mentioned in `triplets` are
upserted standalone so vectors can be attached without new edges.

entity_properties.description:
Client-supplied literal property values to attach to entities in this
commit. Entities are matched by (type, name) against the triplet endpoints
(and embedding targets) in the same commit; each property's `field` is
resolved against the ontology. This is the ingest side of the typed-value
channel that FILTER/aggregation read.

observed_at.description:
Backfill timestamp (RFC3339). When set, this commit is recorded **as of**
that instant rather than now: every edge's transaction time
(`commit_time_micros`) is stamped with it, and any triplet that does not
carry its own `valid_time` defaults its `valid_time.start` to it. Replaying
history in source-chronological order with `observed_at` set per commit is
what makes `as_of` reads (by date) and the temporal-coverage probe
faithful — without it, a backfill stamps everything "now" and every past
`as_of` returns empty. Omit it for normal live writes (stamped now).

oneOf.description:
Optional compare-and-swap guard evaluated atomically against the head
used to build this commit.

How `entity_properties` (and embedding-only) writes combine with an
entity's already-stored fields. `merge` (the default) keeps prior fields
the commit does not re-send and overlays the ones it does — so an
incremental "just add `h_index`" enrichment never drops the title or
status you set earlier. `replace` makes the commit's field list the
entity's complete attribute set, clearing every field it omits (send it
to authoritatively re-state, prune, or with an empty `properties` list to
clear an entity). Omitted = `merge`.

triplets.description:
The edges to commit. Defaults to empty so a properties-only or
embeddings-only commit needs no placeholder `"triplets": []`; the commit
still requires at least one triplet, embedding, or entity-property overall.

oneOf.description: Explicitly null when the ask produced no original plan.

VocabExportResponse.description:
`GET /v1/search/vocab` — the graph's grounding vocabulary as sorted,
deduped string sections: the canonical input for a decoder-side automaton
(an FST/trie builder consumes byte-sorted streams directly), and the
vocabulary half of an "intelligence to go" export bundle. Derived per
snapshot from the same sources the suggest surface reads — no doc/postings
block reads.

attribute_values.description:
Attribute directory entries as `field=value`, byte-sorted. Present only
when a persisted index run carries the attribute directory.

entities.description: Live entity display names, byte-sorted and deduped.

schema.description:
Ontology schema names (entity types, relations, property fields),
byte-sorted and deduped.

truncated.description: True when any section hit its cap (silent truncation is never OK).

max_segments.description:
When appending the new segment would leave the manifest with more
than this many segments, merge them all into one.

min_tail_commits.description: Compact only when the WAL tail holds at least this many commits.

compacted_commits.description: WAL tail commits folded into the new segment.

compacted_seq.description: Highest commit folded; equals `snapshot.compacted_seq` after success.

merged.description: True when this compaction merged the existing segments into one.

segments.description: Segments referenced by the manifest after this compaction.

skipped.description:
True when the tail was below `min_tail_commits` (or another writer
already compacted past this head) and nothing was written.

WarmBudget.description:
A session's remaining warm budget (reads and bytes), decremented as `prefix`
frames warm the cache. Bounds wasted speculative work per session.

WarmedStats.description:
What a `prefix` frame warmed: new terms diffed in, cache blocks fetched, and
bytes read. `exhausted` is set once the session hits a warm budget.

blocks.description: Cache blocks fetched by this frame.

bytes.description: Bytes read into the cache by this frame.

exhausted.description: `true` once a session budget stopped further warming this frame.

terms.description: New complete terms warmed by this frame (already-warmed terms are skipped).

WidenRelationOp.description:
Add already-declared entity types (by name) to a relation's declared
domain (`add_domain`) and/or range (`add_range`). The relation and every
named type must already exist. Ids already present are skipped, so the op
is idempotent and a re-run is a no-op.

add_domain.description: Entity-type names to add to the relation's domain (source types).

add_range.description: Entity-type names to add to the relation's range (target types).

bearer.description: Stack API key (lbb_sk_test_… / lbb_sk_live_…) or single-mode token

info.description: Object-storage-native temporal graph and hybrid search engine. Public data-plane integrations use `/v1/*`, scoped by `?graph=`/`?branch=` (default `main`) and authenticated with a stack API key (`lbb_sk_test_…` or `lbb_sk_live_…`) or single-mode token as a bearer. Send `Lbb-Version: 2026-07-22` to pin this beta-breaking contract.
